This did not make the place less marvelous, but it made it easier to travel in.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into question-and-answer exposition and orderly understanding.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: Learned Oz through careful questioning

options.world_noticed_signal: The old woman's answers came more readily when you asked in that plain order. Even the Munchkins seemed to listen as if learning their own country anew.

options.label: B
options.text: You ask whatever most puzzles you first, following wonder where it leads.
options.outcome: You asked what most puzzled you each moment, as children often do when marvels are thick about them. Were all witches wicked, and what was Kansas to these people, and whether Oz was a man or something else entirely? The questions did not come in a fine order, but they came honestly, and honest questions are often the best kind. The old woman answered them with patience, and from that lively wandering talk the shape of the country still emerged. Oz remained strange, but no longer quite so far away from understanding.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes lively, curious, and child-centered in its exposition.

npc_dispositions.shift: Amused and warm toward Dorothy's curiosity.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1: Pursued living curiosity about Oz

options.world_noticed_signal: The old woman smiled more than once before she answered. The strange land seemed fuller, not smaller, for all the questions you asked of it.

options.label: C
options.text: You press at once for whether Oz truly has the power to send you home.
options.outcome: All the rest seemed of less importance than one thing, so you pressed at once for whether Oz truly had the power to send you home. This gave your curiosity a sharp point, and every answer was measured against Kansas. The old woman did not blame you for it. Indeed, she answered more gravely after that, for she saw that your questions were not idle but born of longing. Thus the talk of witches and cities became, for you, chiefly a way of finding out whether hope was real or only another queer thing in Oz.
options.tonal_effect: Narration narrows wonder toward urgent hope and homesick insistence.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made_and_kept +1: Kept Kansas foremost in every question

options.world_noticed_signal: The mention of Kansas seemed to quiet the little company again. Even the white chalk words on the slate looked more like marching orders than magic.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Witch of the North tells Dorothy to follow the yellow brick road to Emerald City.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup: Toto hides under the bed and the house begins to shake.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: For one little moment you were only a girl in a shaking room, and what your body did first told its own story.

options.label: A
options.text: I move at once, before I can be more afraid.
options.narration_adjustment: So Dorothy sprang after Toto almost before the cry had finished sounding in her ears. Fear was there, to be sure, but it had no time to root her to the floor.

options.label: B
options.text: I freeze a breath, listening to the wind.
options.narration_adjustment: So Dorothy stood still for just one frightened breath while the wind screamed around the house. Then she moved, because the little dog could not be left where he was.

options.label: C
options.text: I look first for Toto and the trap door together.
options.narration_adjustment: So Dorothy's eyes flew from the bed to the trap door and back again. Love and danger stood in two corners of the same room, and she tried to hold both in mind at once.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Setup: the house has lifted and Toto is back in Dorothy's arms.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: When the floor ceased leaping for a moment, Toto was the only familiar thing left in all the house.

options.label: A
options.text: I hold him close and whisper that he is safe.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy held the little black dog close against her and spoke to him softly, though the wind was too loud for words to matter much. Still, kindness often helps whether it is heard or not.

options.label: B
options.text: I set him down, but keep one hand on him.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy set Toto beside her, but her hand remained upon his silky back. She meant to be sensible, yet she would not let him feel alone in that queer ride.

options.label: C
options.text: I tell myself he is safe and look about.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was glad enough that Toto was safe, but after one quick touch she made herself look at the room instead. There is a kind of courage that begins by turning the eyes outward.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation: after shutting the trap door in the flying house.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The dark room, the swaying floor, the bed, the stove, and the table were all still there, and a frightened child may notice such things or not, as she chooses.

options.label: A
options.text: I notice what in the room may help me.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy looked carefully about her and saw that the house, though queerly carried, was still a house with useful things in it. That thought steadied her more than any grand speech could have done.

options.label: B
options.text: I sit still and let the fear pass through.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy sat quite still upon the floor and waited to see what would happen. She had no plan, but waiting calmly was itself a kind of plan when nothing else could be done.

options.label: C
options.text: I keep glancing at the shut trap door.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's eyes returned again and again to the trap door she had just closed. It was only a square in the floor now, yet it seemed to hold all the danger of the house in one place.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation: Dorothy opens the door onto Oz.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The green country lay before you so bright and beautiful that even homesickness had to stand aside for a breath.

options.label: A
options.text: I step out a little to see it better.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy stepped forward a little, with Toto at her heels, that she might better see the green banks and gorgeous flowers. Wonder drew her on as naturally as sunlight draws a flower open.

options.label: B
options.text: I stay in the doorway and look carefully.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy remained in the doorway while she looked about her. The country was beautiful enough, but beauty in a strange land is best examined before it is trusted.

options.label: C
options.text: I look back once into the house first.
options.narration_adjustment: Before giving herself wholly to the new sight, Dorothy glanced back into the little house that had carried her there. It was a poor comfort, but it was the last piece of Kansas she possessed.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Escalation: the Munchkins call Dorothy a sorceress.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: To be bowed to as a noble sorceress was a very queer thing for a little girl who had never killed anything in her life.

options.label: A
options.text: I shrink a little from the title.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy drew back a little at the grand title, for it did not fit her at all. She was too honest to wear it easily, even for a moment.

options.label: B
options.text: I stand still and let them finish.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not know what to make of the speech, so she stood quietly and heard it out. There is often wisdom in letting a strange land explain itself before one answers.

options.label: C
options.text: I lift my chin, though I know it is nonsense.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy knew there must be some mistake, yet she lifted her chin and met their gaze all the same. If these people meant to tell her something useful, she would not spoil it by fluttering too much.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Escalation: after the title of sorceress is spoken and before the silver shoes are discussed.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Grand mistakes may be denied, used, or gently turned aside, and each way has its own color.

options.label: A
options.text: I say plainly that they are mistaken.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy answered plainly, because plain truth was easiest in her mouth. The little people seemed no less astonished, but they listened all the better for hearing honesty.

options.label: B
options.text: I answer politely and ask what they mean.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not seize the title, nor did she strike it away. She turned it gently into a question, which is often the neatest path through confusion.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat: the Witch balances her cap on her nose and counts before it becomes a slate.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The cap upon the end of the old woman's nose was so solemn and so foolish a sight that it invited one kind of face or another.

options.label: A
options.text: I cannot help smiling at the cap.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy smiled through her tears, for the thing was too queer not to be a little funny. The old woman did not seem offended, which proves she was a good witch indeed.

options.label: B
options.text: I watch very solemnly for what comes next.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy watched with great seriousness while the old woman counted. In a strange country, even absurd magic may carry very important news.

options.label: C
options.text: I blink, not knowing whether to laugh or fear.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy hardly knew what face to make of it, and so she only stared. The best part of Oz was that such staring was often rewarded by an answer.

posture_shifts.placement: During Twist beat: the Witch explains the road to Emerald City and the dangers beyond.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: A long road may be heard as a promise, a burden, or simply the next thing to do.

options.label: A
options.text: I listen as if every word may save me.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy listened with all her heart, because in Oz one could not afford to waste even one useful word. The road seemed long, but it also seemed possible.

options.label: B
options.text: I hear only that Kansas is still far away.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy listened, but every sentence was measured chiefly by how far it left Kansas from her still. The road was real enough; so was the ache beneath hearing of it.

options.label: C
options.text: I look toward the road while she speaks.
options.narration_adjustment: Even while the old woman spoke, Dorothy's eyes sought the yellow bricks. It is easier to bear a hard instruction when one can already see the beginning of it.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Aunt Em knows only that a cyclone is coming and the cellar is the refuge. Dorothy knows the same. No one knows the house will be carried to Oz.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Immediate alarm and rescue instinct inside a poor Kansas house.
canon_boundaries_active: Dorothy does not reach the cellar in time.
Toto cannot speak.
The house must rise by cyclone force.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts to save Aunt Em directly
Player attempts to prevent the cyclone entirely
Player tries to make Toto verbally understand commands

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy does not know where she is going or how the house stays aloft. She can only react to the room, the wind, and Toto.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Fear turning into endurance and practical control during the long flight.
canon_boundaries_active: Toto falls toward the trap door and is rescued.
Dorothy survives the flight by waiting it out.
The house lands safely enough for Dorothy to wake unharmed.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to climb out of the airborne house
Player attempts to fly or steer the house
Player tries to reach Aunt Em through the trap door

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The Munchkins know the East Witch is dead and their land is freed. Dorothy knows nothing of Oz. The Witch of the North knows geography and magical basics but not Kansas.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Plain wonder mixed with social confusion and the shock of accidental killing.
canon_boundaries_active: The Munchkins mistake Dorothy for a sorceress.
The East Witch's body disappears, leaving the silver shoes.
The Witch of the North remains good and truthful within her knowledge.
freeform_risk_areas: Player claims real magical powers Dorothy does not have
Player attempts to revive the East Witch
Player refuses the silver shoes in a way that breaks canon transfer

available_objects: yellow brick road visible at distance
scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The Witch of the North knows Oz's location and the broad dangers of the lands, but she has never seen Oz and cannot guarantee his nature. Dorothy still does not know how to get home.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Homesick grief relieved by gentle comic magic, then reshaped into quest resolve.
canon_boundaries_active: The slate message must direct Dorothy to Emerald City.
The Witch cannot accompany Dorothy.
The road to Emerald City is paved with yellow brick.
The Witch's kiss gives real protection.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to force the Witch to teleport Dorothy home
Player insists on crossing the desert directly
Player rejects the road in a way that halts the quest spine

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Dorothy does not yet know the Scarecrow is alive or can speak. She only sees a queer figure by the road.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Hopeful departure, first loneliness on the road, and the beginning of companion-making.
canon_boundaries_active: Dorothy must leave the Munchkin country on the yellow brick road.
The Scarecrow encounter must remain unresolved at session end.
Toto remains with Dorothy.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to recruit the Scarecrow fully within this session
Player attempts to abandon the yellow brick road permanently
Player claims knowledge Dorothy cannot yet have about the Scarecrow

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: As Dorothy cries toward the cellar, the trap door bangs against its frame and Toto comes out from under the bed only after hearing fear in her voice, springing against her skirt just before the house lifts.
paths.current_session_echo: Munchkin Landing scene: when the Witch of the North asks whether Dorothy came alone, Dorothy first looks toward the cellar door left behind in memory before she gathers Toto, and her first homesick line names Aunt Em before Kansas.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: if Dorothy hesitates near the cornfield, the narrator notes that she has already once tried to do the proper thing first, and the Scarecrow's first gratitude line after being freed becomes, "You were good to look at me at all."
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 Homecoming: Aunt Em reunion is framed in plainer, duty-colored language; Dorothy runs to her at once and the narration stresses that she has come back to the call she first tried to answer.
paths.defining_line_captured: You tried to be the good child before you were the brave one.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Toto's timing changes: he does not reach Dorothy until after she has turned toward the cellar, making obedience visibly precede attachment.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through the order of action in narration, the banging trap door, and Toto's delayed physical leap against Dorothy's skirt.

npc_shifts.direction: bond framed through trusted authority and home-duty

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1: Answered Aunt Em's command in crisis

resets: The audible banging of the trap door drops after the cyclone sequence because it belongs only to the Kansas room.
persists: cyclone_departure_mode=attempted_cellar_first persists as Dorothy's remembered first instinct under danger.
Aunt Em bond framing toward authority and return persists into Session 6 reunion texture.
escalates: Repeated road-faithful choices stack with this opening obedience and make later authority figures more trusting.
Repeated home-true choices stack with this opening duty and make the final return narration plainer and swifter.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy catches Toto by his silky body and is holding him in both arms when the floor throws her down halfway to the trap door.
paths.current_session_echo: Silver Shoes transfer scene: when the Witch of the North tells Dorothy to take the shoes, Toto is already quiet in her arms, and the little old woman answers Dorothy as one who has kept hold of the only friend she brought from home.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow first-contact scene: the narrator can frame Dorothy's pause or approach as the same habit that made her try to save Toto and safety together; the Scarecrow reads her as careful rather than fearful.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 Farewell: Dorothy's goodbyes balance home-longing with tenderness toward companions, and Aunt Em's reunion lands with a small note that Dorothy has learned to carry love and practical sense together.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would not go safe if going safe meant going alone.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Toto is visibly secured before lift-off, so the player sees Dorothy succeed in one half of her plan even though the cellar is lost.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through Toto trembling in Dorothy's arms, the halfway distance to the trap door, and the fall happening with him already pressed against her.

world_state_delta.inventory: Toto secured in arms at lift-off

npc_shifts.direction: bond framed as loving but not blindly obedient

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: Secured Toto in immediate danger

resets: The exact halfway position to the trap door clears after the cyclone scene because it is not reusable geography.
persists: cyclone_departure_mode=toto_then_cellar persists as Dorothy's first balanced crisis pattern.
escalates: Repeated kind-hearted choices stack with this balanced rescue and make companions describe Dorothy as both sensible and good.
Repeated wonder-lingering or home-true choices later bend this balance toward marvel or return in farewell scenes.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Dorothy drags Toto out from the dust under the bed and has him safe against her breast before she remembers the cellar at all; then the house rises before she can stand.
paths.current_session_echo: Munchkin Greeting scene: when the Munchkins bow, Dorothy clutches Toto closer than before and answers their praise with sharper homesick feeling, because the little dog is the one thing she saved by her own sudden choice.
paths.next_session_payoff: Scarecrow Pole scene: if Dorothy leaves the road for him, the move feels like a clear continuation of her first Oz instinct; if she does not, the narrator can mark the road as already asking her to deny her own nature.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 Homecoming: Dorothy's reunion with Aunt Em remains loving, but the narration notes that she returns as one whose first law has often been love before order.
paths.defining_line_captured: When love called, even the cyclone had to wait its turn.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The cellar is visibly forgotten until after Toto is rescued, making attachment the unmistakable first action.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through Dorothy crawling into dust and shadow, pulling Toto free first, and only then remembering Aunt Em and the ladder below.

npc_shifts.direction: bond framed through longing rather than obedience

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Chose attachment over procedure

resets: The dust beneath the bed and the sightline to the cellar clear after the cyclone because they are single-scene particulars.
persists: cyclone_departure_mode=toto_above_all persists as Dorothy's first fierce attachment marker.
escalates: Repeated contrary-to-wickedness or immediate-aid choices stack with this path and make later compassionate interruptions feel more natural and more strongly rewarded.
Repeated wonder-lingering or companion-saving choices make Session 6 farewells fuller and harder to leave.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: The Munchkins stop bowing so low, the bells on their blue hats grow still, and the Witch of the North answers Dorothy with direct talk of deserts, directions, and Oz instead of more ceremony.
paths.current_session_echo: Boq Hospitality scene: Boq speaks of the road in plain practical terms and points with his hand while naming the yellow brick road, instead of lingering over Dorothy as a sorceress.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 Emerald approach: Dorothy asks the Scarecrow and later the Tin Woodman shorter, more practical questions about distance and route, and the party's road talk centers on getting to Oz rather than on what Oz may be like.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz reveal: Dorothy is quicker to turn from spectacle to the plain problem of being sent home, pressing Oz with practical urgency once his fraud is known.
paths.defining_line_captured: Wonder had no chance against a homesick question.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The scene visibly shifts from reverent bowing to useful explanation.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through the bells going still, smaller bows, and the Witch of the North changing topic from praise to directions.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1: Began seeking a route home immediately

resets: Boq's hosting posture resets after Session 1 because his house scene does not recur.
persists: munchkin_response_mode=practical_pity persists for the remainder of Session 1 as the social lens on Dorothy.
Dorothy's plain-homeward questioning pattern persists into Oz-facing scenes.
escalates: Repeated home-true questioning compounds into stronger practical pressure on Oz and Glinda later.
Repeated candid questioning makes explanatory NPCs answer Dorothy more directly across sessions.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: One blue-hatted Munchkin steps back, the little bells give a frightened tinkle, and the Witch of the North laughs kindly and answers more frankly because Dorothy has named the scene's moral strangeness aloud.
paths.current_session_echo: Silver Shoes scene: when the dead Witch's feet vanish, the Munchkins watch Dorothy more nervously than before, and Boq's first welcome carries extra ceremony, as if he means not to offend a bold little power.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 first companion talk: the Scarecrow is quicker to trust Dorothy's plain speech when he learns she says what she thinks, and later companion banter around Oz carries more room for calling humbug by its name.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz reveal: Dorothy's condemnation of false greatness pays off cleanly; Oz becomes more meek and self-excusing if this bluntness has been established early.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would sooner offend a whole country than call a hard thing good.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The Munchkins become visibly startled while the Witch of the North becomes visibly more amused and frank.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through one man stepping back, the bells tinkling, and the Witch of the North answering with a laugh instead of solemn reverence.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: Named the accidental killing as troubling

resets: The Munchkins' immediate flinch at Dorothy's tone clears after the East-country farewell.
persists: munchkin_response_mode=startled_reverence persists within Session 1 social reactions.
Early moral bluntness persists as a readable trait for later authority confrontations.
escalates: Repeated contrary-to-wickedness choices compound into sterner narration and sharper villain reactions.
Repeated blunt truth-telling makes false authorities lose ceremonial hold more quickly.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The Munchkins bow lower, their faces ease, and the Witch of the North takes command of the explanation in calm order, unfolding the matter question by question.
paths.current_session_echo: Boq Hospitality scene: Boq receives Dorothy with fuller ceremony, brings out supper with greater confidence, and speaks as though hosting an honored guest who knows proper manners.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 road-to-city scenes: orderly helpers such as farmhouse folk and later the Guardian of the Gates are primed to read Dorothy as civil and proper, smoothing formal exchanges.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 and Session 6: lawful guidance figures, especially the Guardian and Glinda, offer clearer instruction and less stiffness because Dorothy has a pattern of meeting forms properly.
paths.defining_line_captured: Courtesy became your first road through Oz.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The Munchkins' fear visibly settles into hospitality, and the Witch of the North visibly assumes orderly authority.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through lower bows, relieved faces, and the explanation arriving in neat sequence rather than startled bursts.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1: Sought guidance through proper courtesy

resets: Boq's supper-table generosity clears after Session 1 because the host scene is local and complete.
persists: munchkin_response_mode=formal_hospitality persists through the East-country departure.
Dorothy's courteous-seeker pattern persists for later gatekeepers and good rulers.
escalates: Repeated road-faithful conduct compounds into warmer responses from rule-bound NPCs.
Repeated proper asking builds a story-long reputation for being safe to guide.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: The Munchkins stop weeping and stare at Dorothy with rounder eyes, and the Witch of the North gives her a grave approving look after Dorothy declares she will make Oz send her home.
paths.current_session_echo: Road Departure scene: Dorothy steps onto the yellow brick road with a quicker stride, and her first look toward the Emerald City is framed as a claim to be pressed rather than a favor to be begged.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 audience build-up: companion talk about Oz carries more challenge than awe, and when the great city comes in sight Dorothy's questions sharpen toward what Oz can do and why he has not done it already.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 return framing: if wonder later tempts her, this early demanding homeward spirit makes the final choice to leave Oz feel like the completion of a long-held claim.
paths.defining_line_captured: You did not mean to ask greatness kindly if greatness had wronged you.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The quest's emotional frame changes from petition to challenge.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through the Munchkins' widened eyes, the Witch's grave approval, and Dorothy's firmer departure posture on the road.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1: Declared intent to press Oz for return

resets: The Munchkins' widened stare clears once Dorothy has left their country.
persists: future_orientation=home_demanding persists as Dorothy's stated spirit toward Oz.
journey_tone=challenging_authority persists into authority encounters.
escalates: Repeated pressure on authority compounds into Oz becoming more evasive and explanatory.
Repeated home-first declarations make the final return feel cleaner and more inevitable.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: The Witch of the North gives a small satisfied nod, the Munchkins murmur among themselves approvingly, and the yellow brick road is described as a proper errand set in order.
paths.current_session_echo: Kiss and Departure scene: the Witch's kiss lands in a calmer mood, and Dorothy looks down the road as though it were a thing to be followed faithfully rather than tested.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 gate and audience scenes: formal structures, from inns to palace procedures, feel less alien to Dorothy, and orderly NPCs answer her with smoother guidance.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 Glinda counsel: lawful good magic meets less resistance; Glinda's explanation of the Silver Shoes lands as the final proper instruction in a chain Dorothy has honored from the start.
paths.defining_line_captured: You trusted the road before you trusted the country.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The road itself becomes a visibly trusted object, and the surrounding NPCs respond with approval instead of surprise.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through the Witch's nod, the Munchkins' approving murmur, and the narration treating the road as a settled proper course.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1: Committed to the Emerald City route

resets: The Munchkins' approving murmur clears after the departure beat.
persists: future_orientation=home_faithful persists as Dorothy's declared quest spirit.
journey_tone=road_faithful persists into route and gate scenes.
escalates: Repeated road-faithful choices compound into easier passage with formal helpers.
Repeated home-true choices make farewell scenes plainer and less tempted by delay.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The Witch of the North answers Dorothy's questions more fully, one Munchkin smiles through his tears, and the flowers, birds, and green banks are described with nearer, brighter attention once Dorothy admits wonder beside homesickness.
paths.current_session_echo: Road Departure scene: Dorothy looks more than once from the yellow bricks to the fields beside them, and the leave-taking lingers a little over colors and queer beauty before movement resumes.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening travel: descriptions of the road, fields, and first companions widen; Dorothy asks more questions that are not strictly necessary, and the Scarecrow receives a more curious first study.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 6 farewell: goodbyes grow richer and more difficult, and Aunt Em's reunion carries a faint note that Dorothy has brought back more wonder in her heart than she left with.
paths.defining_line_captured: Home still pulled you hardest, but wonder was allowed to walk beside it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The scene visibly opens outward into fuller marvel instead of narrowing only toward escape.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through extra answers from the Witch, the smiling Munchkin, and the narration lingering on flowers, birds, and green banks.

npc_shifts.direction: more eager to share local knowledge

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: secrets_discovered +1: Chose to learn Oz as well as escape it

resets: The immediate brightness of the East-country flowers clears at the next location change.
persists: future_orientation=home_with_wonder persists as Dorothy's mixed motive.
journey_tone=wonder_lingering persists into scenic description and question-answer exchanges.
escalates: Repeated wonder-lingering choices compound into fuller scenic listings and richer farewells.
Repeated curiosity choices make explanatory NPCs volunteer more lore and local detail.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Session-end tableau: Dorothy remains in the yellow road while the corn whispers and Toto gives a doubtful bark; the Scarecrow is held at a distance by her careful looking.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening: the Scarecrow's first movement is noticed before Dorothy touches him, and his first line after she tests him becomes cautious and grateful, with more question-answer exchange before rescue is completed.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: bright uncertainty. First image: yellow morning on the road and a blue-clad figure among the green corn. First character: Dorothy, with Toto sniffing and the Scarecrow giving the smallest wink. Immediate stakes: decide whether the queer thing is a person to be helped or a trick to be avoided.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz reveal: the Scarecrow tends to treat Dorothy as someone who thinks before acting, so his loyalty expresses itself through counsel and agreement rather than impulsive advocacy.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the road wait while you decided what sort of creature straw might be.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The distance between Dorothy and the Scarecrow is preserved into the cut, making caution the visible shape of the hook.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through Dorothy staying on the road, the cornfield remaining unentered, and the Scarecrow being watched rather than approached.

npc_shifts.direction: first impression begins with measured attention

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Paused to assess before helping a stranger

resets: The frozen session-end pause itself clears as soon as Session 2 begins and action resumes.
persists: scarecrow_first_contact_mode=observe_first persists into the first Scarecrow exchange.
Scarecrow's first impression of Dorothy as careful persists into companion trust texture.
escalates: If later choices also pause to assess before helping, Scarecrow reads Dorothy as deliberate and becomes more plan-forward than heart-forward.
If later choices swing toward mercy after this caution, the contrast itself becomes visible in Scarecrow dialogue.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Session-end tableau: Dorothy's silver shoes keep clicking on the yellow bricks while Toto looks back toward the field, and the corn makes a small dry sound behind her.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening: the Scarecrow must call or move to stop Dorothy from leaving him, so his first gratitude carries relief at being noticed after delay, and Dorothy's rescue begins from road-faithfulness being interrupted.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: forward motion troubled by conscience. First image: the yellow brick road stretching ahead while a patch of green corn behind it stirs oddly. First character: Dorothy walking, with Toto stopping and looking back. Immediate stakes: whether to keep faith with the road or answer the queer persistence behind her.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz reveal: if Dorothy has often chosen efficiency, the Scarecrow's defense of her is sadder and more earned, because he knows she did not stop for him at once and yet came back.
paths.defining_line_captured: You chose the road, though the road had already begun to test that choice.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The hook shows Dorothy physically continuing on the road instead of stopping, making efficiency visible before the cut.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through the steady click of the silver shoes, Toto's backward attention, and the Scarecrow left behind in the sounding corn.

npc_shifts.direction: future gratitude begins from delay rather than immediate rescue

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1: Kept to the yellow brick road without delay

resets: The exact end-beat of walking past the field clears when Session 2 reopens the moment.
persists: scarecrow_first_contact_mode=pass_by_first persists into Scarecrow's first spoken gratitude.
Scarecrow remembers the delay because he witnesses it directly.
escalates: Repeated speed-over-aid choices compound into Scarecrow offering plans less boldly and testing Dorothy's loyalty more quietly.
Repeated road-faithful choices make formal helpers trust Dorothy while vulnerable companions warm more slowly.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Session-end tableau: Dorothy has already stepped off the yellow brick road, the corn brushes her sleeves, and the Scarecrow seems more like somebody with every step she takes toward him.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening: Dorothy begins close at the pole, so the Scarecrow's first line can be immediate, eager, and warmly grateful; his trust starts high because she came before certainty.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: cheerful wonder with quick kindness. First image: green corn standing high about Dorothy and the Scarecrow's painted face close at hand. First character: the Scarecrow, almost alive already. Immediate stakes: free him at once or discover too late that a helpless creature has been waiting long for a friend.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 4 Oz reveal: this path gives the Scarecrow the strongest ground to advocate for Dorothy, because she helped him before he had even proved he was worth the delay.
paths.defining_line_captured: You left the safe road for a straw man before he had even spoken.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Dorothy is already in the cornfield at the cut, making immediate compassion the visible final image.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Through Dorothy leaving the road, the corn touching her sleeves, and the Scarecrow seeming more person than object as distance closes.

npc_shifts.direction: strong first-impression gratitude primed by immediate approach

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: Moved toward vulnerable stranger without delay

resets: The suspended step into the corn clears when Session 2 begins and the encounter unfolds.
persists: scarecrow_first_contact_mode=help_at_once persists into the first rescue exchange.
Scarecrow's memory of immediate approach persists across the full story.
escalates: Repeated immediate-aid choices compound into stronger companion loyalty and warmer camp talk.
Repeated contrary-to-wickedness and kind-hearted choices make Scarecrow become Dorothy's quickest defender in later moral disputes.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If cyclone_departure_mode=attempted_cellar_first when Session 6 reaches Dorothy's return to Aunt Em.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy runs home with simple relief, and the reunion is warm without special emphasis on obedience remembered.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The reunion line notes that Dorothy had tried first to answer Aunt Em's call even before Oz began, and Dorothy goes straight into Aunt Em's arms before speaking of anything she has seen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: State the remembered duty plainly, and then let the little gray kitchen and Aunt Em's arms seem the proper end of a road long followed.

npc_reactions: Aunt Em's relief feels immediate and unquestioned.
Dorothy's body language is direct and homeward, not lingering or dazzled.
environmental_details: Kansas domestic details are noticed before any thought of Oz's colors.
The house feels plain and right rather than poor and changed.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's first spoken line centers on Aunt Em rather than on what has happened.
Any reunion summary omits marvel-first phrasing.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If cyclone_departure_mode=toto_above_all when Session 6 reaches the Aunt Em reunion.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy's return is loving and swift, with no special callback to her first impulsive rescue.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The narration frames Dorothy as coming home with the same heart that once seized Toto before all else, and her reunion carries a soft note that love has always outrun order in her.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Name the feeling directly, and then let the homecoming show that the same little heart which clung to Toto now clung to Kansas again.

npc_reactions: Aunt Em's embrace reads as receiving back an unchanged loving child.
Toto's presence near the reunion gains a little emphasis.
environmental_details: Domestic details share space with the remembered feel of Toto against Dorothy's breast.
The scene feels tender before it feels settled.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's first words may include Toto or home together.
The reunion summary leans on love rather than propriety.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If cyclone_departure_mode=toto_then_cellar when later scenes evaluate Dorothy's crisis habits.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Later narration treats Dorothy's care as generally kind without naming a first pattern.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Companions and narration frame Dorothy as one who tries to keep both safety and affection together, especially in travel decisions and farewells.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the prose show the balance by pairing one practical act with one tender one, and explain it in the plainest terms.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow and Tin Woodman read Dorothy as sensible and kind together.
Good guides answer her as though she can bear both feeling and instruction.
environmental_details: Travel scenes notice road and companion side by side.
Farewell scenes hold home and friendship in one frame.
dialogue_variations: Companions thank Dorothy for remembering both the journey and the friend.
Dorothy's questions often pair a route question with a care question.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has at least one prior Session 1 marker of stopping, balancing, or failing to stop for a vulnerable being before the Scarecrow scene opens.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Scarecrow encounter begins as a fresh curiosity with no narrated comparison.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The narrator briefly compares the Scarecrow moment to Dorothy's earlier treatment of Toto, making her approach, pause, or passing-on feel like a pattern instead of an isolated act.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Use one plain backward-looking sentence, and then go on at once to the straw man and the road before him.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow's first gratitude or uncertainty is shaded by how promptly Dorothy acts.
Toto's behavior can reinforce the pattern by barking, waiting, or following.
environmental_details: The road may feel waiting, or the corn may feel inviting, depending on the pattern.
The distance from road to pole is described with moral weight.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow's first thanks may be immediate, cautious, or relieved.
Dorothy's first question may be practical or tender in tone.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If munchkin_response_mode=practical_pity or Dorothy has multiple home-first practical questions logged by Session 4 event 154-162.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: After Oz is exposed, Dorothy is disappointed and asks again to be sent home.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy presses Oz more quickly and more plainly on the practical matter of Kansas, and Oz retreats into balloons, tools, and delay instead of trying long ceremonial reassurance.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Strip the grandeur away in a few calm strokes, and then let the talk turn at once from humbug to the road home.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes managerial and evasive sooner.
Scarecrow watches Dorothy with practical respect.
environmental_details: The Throne Room props feel less marvelous and more contrived.
Attention lingers on screens, cords, and devices rather than spectacle.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy asks, in effect, how she is to get to Kansas now.
Oz answers with the balloon plan earlier in the exchange.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy chose blunt moral speech in S1_C2 path B or has accumulated two or more contrary-to-wickedness choices by Session 4.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy is shocked by Oz's fraud and speaks with disappointment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Dorothy calls Oz a bad man more directly, and Oz answers in his meek private mode with self-excusing honesty instead of trying to preserve much dignity.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Name the wrong simply, and then let the old man shrink into his ordinary speech without any dark flourish.

npc_reactions: Oz becomes embarrassed and apologetic at once.
Scarecrow either admires Dorothy's plainness or worries for the peace, depending on trust.
environmental_details: The machinery behind the illusion feels smaller and more foolish.
The room loses grandeur quickly.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's rebuke is plainer and shorter.
Oz uses 'my dear' sooner and admits he is a bad Wizard.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If munchkin_response_mode is practical_pity, startled_reverence, or formal_hospitality during Boq's hosting scene in Session 1.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Boq welcomes Dorothy respectfully and offers ordinary Munchkin hospitality.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Boq's welcome changes by mode: practical_pity makes him speak plainly of the road; startled_reverence makes him bow more and choose careful words; formal_hospitality makes him host with orderly confidence and fuller courtesy.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the difference first in how Boq stands and speaks at his own door, and then let the supper table confirm it.

npc_reactions: Boq's posture, candor, and confidence shift by mode.
Nearby Munchkins either relax, hover nervously, or beam with ceremony.
environmental_details: The house feels either homelier, more formal, or more hushed.
The serving of food becomes brisk, careful, or celebratory.
dialogue_variations: Boq either names dangers plainly, uses extra honorific courtesy, or mixes welcome with practical guidance.
His questions to Dorothy become direct, cautious, or ceremonious.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Dorothy has accumulated at least two lawful or road-faithful markers by the time she reaches the Guardian of the Gates.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Guardian is formal, rule-bound, and not unkind while locking on the green spectacles.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Guardian softens after the procedure, explains the city rules more helpfully, and opens the gate without extra stiffness because Dorothy submits as one who respects forms.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the ceremony exact, and then let the kindness appear in one useful explanation and one easier gesture.

npc_reactions: Guardian of the Gates becomes more informative and less stiff.
Palace attendants receive Dorothy as a proper visitor.
environmental_details: The gate passage feels orderly rather than coercive.
The green box and spectacles are described as civic procedure more than menace.
dialogue_variations: The Guardian offers one extra line of guidance about the city.
He uses a warmer courtesy formula after the spectacles are locked on.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If future_orientation is home_demanding, home_faithful, or home_with_wonder when Dorothy faces Oz's fraud and later Glinda's counsel.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Dorothy seeks home with general longing and reacts to new help in a standard way.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: home_demanding makes Dorothy press power to answer for her return; home_faithful makes her accept the next lawful route with steadier trust; home_with_wonder makes her still seek Kansas but ask fuller questions and feel more of Oz's beauty in the loss.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the same wish for Kansas remain fixed, and change only the manner in which Dorothy bears it and speaks it.

npc_reactions: Oz reacts with evasion, procedure, or explanation depending on orientation.
Glinda answers with brisk certainty, approving steadiness, or gentler fullness.
environmental_details: Emerald scenes feel accusatory, orderly, or more lingeringly beautiful.
Farewell spaces either narrow toward departure or widen toward remembrance.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's wording around home changes in force, humility, or curiosity.
Guides answer either the demand, the obedience, or the wondering question.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If journey_tone is challenging_authority, road_faithful, or wonder_lingering when Session 6 reaches farewells and the return home.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Farewells are tender and the return is satisfying.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: challenging_authority makes Dorothy's leaving feel like the completion of a hard-won claim; road_faithful makes it feel like the proper end of a long errand; wonder_lingering makes the parting richer, slower, and a little sadder before Kansas receives her.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the tears plain, and let the difference live in whether the scene hastens, settles, or lingers among bright particulars.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow, Lion, and Glinda part from Dorothy with differing shades of pride, order, or wistfulness.
Aunt Em's reunion carries less or more sense that Dorothy has been altered by wonder.
environmental_details: Farewell descriptions either shorten, steady, or lengthen over colors and embraces.
Kansas either arrives quickly or after one last lingering look backward.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy's last words in Oz are brisker, more dutiful, or more wondering.
Companions answer with practical blessing, proper leave-taking, or fuller sorrow.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If journey_tone=wonder_lingering or Dorothy has chosen curiosity-forward options in early Oz scenes.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Travel description remains functional and brisk.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The narrator gives extra bright particulars at rest beats and first sightings, and NPCs volunteer more local explanation because Dorothy has shown she wishes to know the country as well as pass through it.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Add one or two clear colored things, and then answer Dorothy's question directly before the road goes on.

npc_reactions: Witch of the North and Boq become more explanatory.
Scarecrow receives more curious attention in first contact.
environmental_details: Flowers, birds, fields, and city colors receive fuller listing.
Marvels feel nearer and more child-visible.
dialogue_variations: Dorothy asks one more question than necessity requires.
Guides answer with geography or custom rather than only direction.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If scarecrow_first_contact_mode=observe_first, pass_by_first, or help_at_once at the opening of Session 2.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Scarecrow encounter opens with a standard curious approach and rescue.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: observe_first opens with distance and testing; pass_by_first opens with Dorothy already moving away until the Scarecrow's odd sign arrests her; help_at_once opens close at the pole with immediate eager gratitude from the Scarecrow.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Begin with Dorothy's exact position in relation to the yellow brick road, and let that distance tell the truth before anyone says a word.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is cautious, relieved, or warmly eager depending on mode.
Toto sniffs, looks back, or follows in at once.
environmental_details: The road either waits, continues, or falls behind.
The cornfield either remains at the edge, calls from behind, or closes around Dorothy.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow's first line changes in warmth and urgency.
Dorothy's first question changes from testing to apology to direct concern.
affected_elements.available_options: Available options remain the same, but the first expressive responses in Session 2 should reflect distance, delay, or immediate aid.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If Scarecrow's first impression was help_at_once, observe_first, or pass_by_first, combined with later accumulated kindness or impatience by Session 4.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow supports Dorothy during the Oz reveal with ordinary gratitude and friendship.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: help_at_once plus later kindness makes Scarecrow Dorothy's quickest and strongest advocate; observe_first makes him supportive but more counsel-giving than ardent; pass_by_first makes his support warmer if earned, but touched with remembered relief that she did come back for him.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: When the humbug is exposed, let the straw man remember the pole without naming a ledger, and let his loyalty show in one plain sentence and one quick act.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow's warmth, speed of defense, and confidence in Dorothy vary sharply.
Oz reads the party's unity differently depending on Scarecrow's stance.
environmental_details: The reveal scene feels steadier when Scarecrow stands firmly with Dorothy.
If support is delayed or cautious, the room feels more uncertain.
dialogue_variations: Scarecrow's lines may stress immediate trust, thoughtful agreement, or grateful remembrance.
Dorothy receives either quick affirmation or measured counsel from him.

resets_between_episodes.element: Boq's moment-to-moment hosting posture
resets_between_episodes.why: It belongs to his single supper scene in the East and does not travel beyond that house.

resets_between_episodes.element: The Kansas room's trap door, ladder, and banging frame as active scene props
resets_between_episodes.why: They are tied to the cyclone cut and cannot remain interactable once Dorothy is in Oz.

resets_between_episodes.element: The frozen session-end pause before the Scarecrow speaks
resets_between_episodes.why: It is a retention hook image, not a lasting world condition; Session 2 must convert it into action.

resets_between_episodes.element: Immediate Munchkin crowd spacing and flinch behavior
resets_between_episodes.why: Those precise body reactions belong to the first greeting cluster and resolve once Dorothy departs their gathering.

persists_across_full_story.why: It defines Dorothy's first instinct under danger and can color homecoming, companion trust, and symbolic narration.
It records the first social lens through which Oz receives Dorothy and shapes the tone of East-country aid.
It establishes whether Dorothy seeks home as demand, faithful errand, or longing mixed with wonder, and that meaning belongs to the whole arc.
It affects scenic framing, authority encounters, and the emotional shape of the farewell ending.
Scarecrow directly witnesses it, so it becomes part of his lasting knowledge of Dorothy's character.
Public identity in Oz accumulates from first arrival through Witch-slaying and Glinda's recognition.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated road-faithful obedience to given paths, rules, and proper forms

thresholds.at_2_instances: Orderly NPCs such as Boq or the Guardian answer with one extra useful courtesy and less suspicion.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Formal societies treat Dorothy as reliably proper; gates, audiences, and instructions become smoother and more approving.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The narration consistently frames Dorothy as good, careful, and proper, while false authorities may hide behind procedure longer because they expect compliance.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated immediate aid to vulnerable creatures or delayed strangers

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions thank Dorothy more openly and small acts of gratitude begin to echo in camp talk.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion offer stronger loyalty, quicker help, and warmer body language in danger.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy acquires a story-wide reputation for plain goodness; allies gather readily and farewell scenes become deeply affectionate and hard to leave.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated blunt moral truth spoken against wickedness or false greatness

thresholds.at_2_instances: Wicked or fraudulent figures lose some ceremonial hold and answer Dorothy more defensively.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Companions increasingly look to Dorothy for moral naming when scenes turn confused or unjust.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Narration grows sterner and clearer around Dorothy; villains react sharply, and illusions collapse faster in her presence.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated wonder-lingering curiosity about Oz beyond immediate necessity

thresholds.at_2_instances: Scenic descriptions lengthen slightly and guides volunteer one extra fact or bright detail.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Companions share more of themselves, and the marvelous regions of Oz feel fuller and more child-visible.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Farewells become richly colored and sorrowful, and the final return to Kansas carries a clear note that Dorothy has been changed by marvel.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated home-true insistence on Kansas, Aunt Em, and practical return

thresholds.at_2_instances: Dorothy's questions turn more quickly toward routes, means, and promises of return.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Oz and Glinda scenes answer her with brisker practicality, and the narration grows plainer and more domestic around her wish.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The ending narrows cleanly toward home; Dorothy leaves Oz with little delay, and the reunion lands as the great proper fulfillment of all that came before.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by reminding the reader, in plain words, that Dorothy first turned toward the cellar and the call of home before the storm kept her.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Duty answered before comfort was secured.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy reach the cellar safely.
Do not let Aunt Em return to the room.
Do not let Toto speak or obey like a person.
Do not introduce new escape tools or magic.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may very well mean to do the proper thing first, for Aunt Em had called in earnest.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the floor leaps, the trap door bangs, and the cyclone takes the house before any orderly escape can be finished.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by noting that Dorothy tried to save Toto and safety together whenever danger gave her even a little time.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Love and prudence held in one pair of hands.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy both save Toto and reach the cellar in time.
Do not remove Toto from the house.
Do not alter the cyclone into a magical choice-made event.
Do not add knowledge of Oz.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may try to do the sensible thing that keeps both the little dog and herself from harm.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But one little moment is lost in the reaching, and the house is lifted before the ladder can be gained.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by recalling that when a loved creature was frightened, Dorothy forgot every rule for one quick moment and followed her heart.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Attachment outran procedure.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: impulsive, fiercely protective, defiant of procedure, emotion-first input
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy prevent the house from rising.
Do not let Toto become supernaturally important or magical.
Do not allow Aunt Em to be rescued from below.
Do not let the player choose to remain behind in Kansas.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may fling herself after Toto without stopping to think, and that is very like her loving heart.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the storm is already too great for household plans, and the house rises before any second thought can become an action.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Dorothy cut through marvel whenever Kansas is at stake and ask the plainest useful question first.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Homesick practicality over ceremony.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy know a real route home besides Oz.
Do not let the Munchkins turn hostile.
Do not let Dorothy become a true sorceress.
Do not skip the Witch of the North's explanation.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may put aside all bows and ask at once how she is to get home.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But no one in that little company can send her there directly, so the answer must still lead by way of Oz and the yellow brick road.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having Dorothy name hard things as hard things, even when a whole country would rather call them deliverance.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Plain moral truth against false comfort.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive, defiant, morally blunt, offended input
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy insult the good Witch cruelly or with modern snark.
Do not let the Munchkins attack or banish her.
Do not deny that the East Witch's death freed them.
Do not turn Dorothy's speech into sarcasm.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may speak sharply, for the silver-shod feet beneath the house are a dreadful sight to a little girl.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the Munchkins are too long oppressed to answer her with anger, and the Witch of the North turns the sharpness into frank explanation instead.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by showing Dorothy using courtesy as a practical key that opens frightened people and orderly help.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Proper conduct as the first safe road in a strange land.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy fully accept being a sorceress.
Do not let courtesy solve the home problem immediately.
Do not remove the silver shoes transfer.
Do not make the Witch of the North servile or overawed.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may answer modestly and ask the proper person for guidance, which is a very sensible thing in a queer country.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But good manners cannot shorten the deserts round Oz, so the same hard road must still be explained to her.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Dorothy face greatness as something answerable, not something before which she must only bow.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Home claimed as a right, not begged as a favor.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy refuse the road entirely.
Do not let the Witch of the North accompany her.
Do not let Dorothy know Oz is a humbug yet.
Do not make Dorothy cruel or swaggering.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may very well mean to tell this great Wizard what is due to a little girl cast far from home.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But however boldly she speaks, the only lawful way forward is still the yellow brick road to Emerald City.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by treating the yellow brick road as a comfort whenever Dorothy must go on without certainty.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Trust placed in the given path before the land itself is trusted.
hard_limits: Do not let obedience become passive despair.
Do not let the road itself become magical beyond canon.
Do not allow a shortcut home.
Do not erase Dorothy's homesickness.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may choose to follow the road faithfully, because a plain road is a comfort when all else is strange.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But faithful going cannot spare her the length of the journey, and she must still walk through Oz to reach its ruler.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by lingering a little over bright things whenever Dorothy allows Oz to be seen as well as escaped.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Wonder admitted without surrendering home.
hard_limits: Do not let curiosity replace Dorothy's wish for Kansas.
Do not add lore no one present could know.
Do not turn the scene mystical or metaphor-heavy.
Do not let Dorothy choose to stay in Oz now.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may wish to look well at this queer land even while she longs for home, and that is no wicked thing.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But all such wondering must still travel upon the same yellow brick road, for there is no other way shown.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having Dorothy pause, look twice, and let uncertainty remain visible before kindness chooses its form.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Caution that has not yet hardened into refusal.
hard_limits: Do not let the Scarecrow speak fully before the session ends.
Do not resolve the encounter inside Session 1.
Do not let Dorothy discover his whole nature at a glance.
Do not move the scene away from the road and cornfield.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may stop and study the queer figure, for in Oz it is wise to know what a thing is before laying hands upon it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the moment must remain hanging at session's end, with the corn whispering and the answer not yet given.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting the road pull at Dorothy's feet even when pity begins to pull at her heart.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Efficiency chosen, but not without cost of conscience.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy leave the Scarecrow forever.
Do not let the session resolve whether she returns.
Do not make Toto understand the Scarecrow like a person.
Do not invent a second destination besides Emerald City.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may mean to lose no daylight over every queer sight in Oz, especially when Kansas still lies so far away.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the field keeps its queer claim upon her, and the session must close before the road can prove whether she was right to pass by.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Dorothy leave safety quickly whenever a helpless-looking creature seems to need her.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Compassion before certainty.
hard_limits: Do not let Dorothy fully free the Scarecrow before Session 2.
Do not let the Scarecrow deliver a whole conversation in Session 1.
Do not remove Toto from the scene.
Do not abandon the yellow brick road permanently.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may go straight into the corn at once, because kindness does not commonly wait for certainty.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the session must end with her still upon that approach, so that the next morning may begin with the queer fellow close at hand and not yet fully known.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: At last you have what you set out to gain. You are no longer a little girl standing alone before a strange house in a strange land. You have companions now, and they are true ones. The Scarecrow walks beside you, though he has no brains, and the Tin Woodman goes with you, though he has no heart, and now the great Lion, who is afraid of nearly everything, has chosen your road as well. The Tin Woodman’s jaws are oiled open again, and the sharp smell of the oil still clings to the air, while the yellow brick road runs bright between the dark trees. Toto keeps close to your heels, and that is a comfort. So your journey to the City of Emeralds has fairly begun, and it is plain that you may reach Oz after all. Yet as the four of you stand together in the forest road, with the green city still far away, there is one thing not even a good witch can tell for you: what sort of leader you mean to be on the way.
session_close_design.hook_transition: For one little moment it is enough merely to have friends beside you. Then the forest grows quiet again, and three different needs walk at your elbow as plainly as the road itself.

session_end_choice.choice_question: When you lead your little company onward, whose need do you place first upon the road to the City of Emeralds?

option_a.text: Keep your own errand first, and press on toward Oz as quickly as you can, for Aunt Em and Uncle Henry must be waiting.
option_a.next_session_opens: Next session opens with you urging the company to make speed on the yellow brick road, even when the way grows rough and hungry.

option_b.text: Shape the journey around the Scarecrow, and look first for chances to use his wit and help him win the brains he longs for.
option_b.next_session_opens: Next session opens with you drawing the Scarecrow into each new problem, treating the road as a place to prove what kind of brains he may already have.

option_c.text: Make the company a shelter for the Lion, and lead gently so that fear does not master him before he reaches the Great Oz.
option_c.next_session_opens: Next session opens with you watching the Lion closely, choosing a steadier and kinder pace through the forest and whatever dangers come after it.

session_end_choice.final_line: The yellow brick road waits, and this time it waits for the way you choose to walk it.

q1_entry_point.detail: The session starts at the first live crisis: Toto under the bed, Aunt Em already descending, the trap door open, and the house shaking. No passive Kansas setup precedes the dramatic problem.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 arrives within the cold open at approximately 164 words from the start of the cold open prose.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices change tracked state, not just tone: S1_C1 sets cyclone_departure_mode with Session 6 homecoming payoff; S1_C2 sets munchkin_response_mode with Session 4 Oz-reveal payoff; S1_C3 sets future_orientation/journey_tone with Session 6 farewell-return payoff; S1_C4 sets scarecrow_first_contact_mode with Session 4 Scarecrow loyalty payoff. Consequence maps and cross-session payoff plan are named and aligned.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Before the first branch, Dorothy's internal stake is clear: save Toto and reach safety, with home attachment embedded through Toto and Aunt Em. The why is legible immediately.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S1_C1 has explicit later payoff in Session 6 homecoming texture, and S1_C4 has explicit next-session and Session 4 loyalty payoffs. Early decisions are visibly planned to matter later.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The breath beat exists, but it lands at 8:40-10:00 in the beat map, not between minutes 10-13 as required by this checklist. Phase 3/4 beat timing fails the exact window. Revision: shift the cap-and-slate comic wonder beat later so the release lands inside 10:00-13:00 before the Twist compounds.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S1_C3. Values in tension: duty to home, trust in guidance, curiosity toward wonder. No option is objectively correct; each reflects a legitimate human value.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: The user exits carrying ANTICIPATION. Session 1 ends on the unresolved Scarecrow decision, not on a closed plot beat or unclear state.

q10_talkability.detail: S1_C1 and S1_C4 are immediately talkable: save Toto versus obey Aunt Em, and stop for the Scarecrow versus keep to the road. A friend would plausibly ask, 'What did you choose?'

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned modern or non-Baum-like elements appear in generated prose/design text used as session prose. Found: 'moral gray' in session architecture twist rationale and Q-facing labels; 'identity,' 'methodology,' 'future orientation,' and 'retention hook' are meta molds inside player-facing session-close package; most critically, session_close_design resolution prose introduces non-canon companions and events for Session 1 ('The Scarecrow walks beside you... the Tin Woodman... the great Lion...'). Phase 7 fails. Revision: replace the entire session_close_design block with the actual Session 1 close on the unresolved Scarecrow choice and remove all meta/player-invisible modern phrasing from any generated prose fields.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: Trailing similes appear in action lines: cold open 'the floor leaped under you like a boat upon rough water'; S1_C4A 'as if reminding you they had not moved'; multiple world-noticed signals use trailing comparison structures. Phase 5 prose fails. Revision: remove action-line similes and restate the physical fact plainly in Baum's matter-of-fact mode.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: No clear instances of the banned molds 'It's not X, it's Y,' 'No X. No Y. Just Z.,' or the specified contrast scaffolds dominate the generated prose.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: The cold open strongly reflects Baum through plain marvel delivery and sequential connective chaining. Random outcomes such as S1_C1B and S1_C3B also show direct emotional naming and practical consequence. However, the session_close_design prose is not attributable to Baum for Session 1 because it jumps to later companions and uses a recap-like, externally framed cadence inconsistent with the actual chapter position. Phase 7 fails. Revision: rewrite session close in true Session 1 chronology and keep attribution anchored in plain storybook progression rather than retrospective ensemble summary.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: Not all 14 voice-lock points pass. The major failures are Point 7 Paragraph Architecture/continuous experience integrity at the session close, Point 11 Narrator Compliance due to the wrong-session recap feel in session_close_design, and Point 10/continuity-adjacent voice authenticity because the close no longer sounds like this chapter of Baum. Return to Phase 7 and replace the close with the Scarecrow hook.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Most Dorothy, Witch of the North, and Munchkin prose matches Baum's diction clusters well. The failing element is the session_close_design prose, whose diction belongs to a later ensemble road scene and not Dorothy's Session 1 internal/narrative position. Phase 7 fails. Revision: align final prose with Session 1 diction markers—yellow brick road, cornfield, queer figure, Toto, and immediate homesick quest framing only.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: session_close_design.resolution_prose; the paragraph summarizes a later-party state outside Session 1 and breaks continuous session architecture.
session_close_design.session_end_choice; the choice frame concerns Scarecrow/Tin Woodman/Lion leadership priorities that belong to a later ensemble state, not Session 1 character differentiation.
session_close_design.resolution_prose; narrator behaves like a retrospective session recap rather than Baum's in-scene third-person progression for this chapter position.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: The specific failing element is session_close_design, which places Dorothy with Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion together on a forest road in Session 1. That violates the session map and narrative canon for events 1-49. Phase 7 fails. Revision: replace the close with Dorothy on the yellow brick road seeing the Scarecrow in the cornfield, and end on that unresolved decision only.

q18_character_truth.detail: Within the actual Session 1 choice prose, Aunt Em, Toto, Dorothy, the Witch of the North, Boq, and the Munchkins behave within StoryGuard character rules. No NPC is made psychologically modern or canon-breaking.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible: Munchkin bows shift by response mode, Toto timing changes by S1_C1, Witch/Boq warmth changes by S1_C2 and S1_C3, and the Scarecrow opening mode is prepared for Session 2. Environmental, NPC, and symbolic-thematic reactivity are all present.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: State changes specified in branching choices are mirrored in the consequence map: cyclone_departure_mode, munchkin_response_mode, future_orientation, journey_tone, scarecrow_first_contact_mode, alignment shifts, and ledger entries all carry through consistently.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant persistent state changes have in-world acknowledgments written in Phase 5. Signals are subtle, physical, and in voice: bells growing still, trap door banging, corn brushing sleeves, the Witch's nod, Toto's posture, and the road's felt pull.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices have three defining lines each, all under 20 words, provocative, and non-spoilery. Count matches 12 total defining lines.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: Each branching choice spans lawful/neutral/chaotic distinctly, and no single register is systematically advantaged in the written consequences. The alignment spread is clear and balanced.

revision_instructions.instruction: Move the Breath beat later so the cap-and-slate reward lands between minutes 10 and 13 before the Twist.
Replace the entire session close with the actual Session 1 Scarecrow hook and remove meta-modern phrasing from any generated prose fields.
Strip trailing similes from action lines and restate those beats in plain declarative Baum-style prose.
Rewrite the closing prose in true Session 1 chronology so the voice remains attributable to Baum at this exact chapter position.
Repair the failing 14-point audit items by replacing the non-Session-1 close with an in-scene Scarecrow-ending that preserves narrator compliance and chapter-true architecture.
Realign final diction to Session 1-specific Baum markers: yellow brick road, cornfield, queer figure, Toto, and immediate homeward purpose only.
Remove the later-companion forest-road close and end Session 1 on Dorothy's unresolved Scarecrow decision beside the cornfield.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The dramatic energy for Session 2 does not begin with travel progress, camp routine, or obstacle logistics. Events 50-95 are readable and charming, but for a new user they function mostly as connective road business: camp setup, ditch crossing, Kalidah warning, another gulf, a tree bridge, river crossing, Scarecrow stranded and rescued, flower admiration, poppy danger, Lion rescue, farmhouse hospitality, Emerald City arrival, spectacle procedure, palace lodging, and ceremonial delay before the audience. Stakes exist in pieces, but they are episodic and repeatedly resolved before the player reaches the session's true engine. The first paragraph-level moment where a new user can immediately feel the session's central stakes, without needing future knowledge, is Dorothy's audience with Oz: she enters alone, faces the Great and Terrible Wizard, asks to go home, and is told she must kill the Wicked Witch of the West or never see Kansas again. That is the first clean YES under the 60-second stakes test because the desire, obstacle, authority, and consequence are all present at once. Everything before that is cut because it delays the core dramatic question of this session: can the company endure the road's dangers and win help, only to learn the terrible price of it? The cut does remove useful quest texture and some companion warmth, so the cold open must quickly reintroduce that Dorothy has traveled far with friends, that she longs for Kansas, and that Oz is a feared ruler in a green city. But those facts should come through immediate action and plain question-answer framing, not through resumed setup.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: Chapter 5, Dorothy enters the Throne Room and meets Oz as a great Head (Event 96).
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Events 50-95 cut; 46 story events eliminated before session start.
