options.tonal_effect: Narration emphasizes controlled pressure and practical escalation.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1 shaped anger into leverage

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You are sorry it has come to this, but promises still matter.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not enjoy the rising quarrel, for she would rather have had the matter settled kindly and in good order. Yet she could not deny that promises are binding things, and delay after such service was poor conduct in any ruler. Therefore her sorrow and her firmness stood side by side. This gave the scene a grave quality, as if the company were not merely angry but justified. Even their impatience felt proper.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes grave, orderly, and morally clear.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made_and_kept +1 affirmed the binding force of promises

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The pressure rises toward the Lion's roar and the fall of the screen.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The screen falls and the little old man is revealed where terror had been expected.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Then the screen crashes down, and the Great and Terrible Oz proves to be a little old man. It is such a queer sight that wonder and disappointment arrive together.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What sort of astonishment takes hold of you first?

options.label: A
options.text: You stare because the truth is queerer than the lie.
options.outcome: Dorothy could only stare. A great Head would have been marvelous, and a lovely Lady or a terrible Beast would have been marvelous also; but this little bald old man behind a screen was marvel of another kind. It was plain, absurd, and wonderful all at once. The surprise did not drive thought away. Rather, it invited thought, for now every former appearance of Oz had to be fitted around this common figure like bright paper around a very small parcel.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into gentle comic deflation and plain marvel.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1 received the humbug reveal with wonder

options.world_noticed_signal: The fallen screen lay like a broken piece of grandeur, and the little man blinked in the middle of it.

options.label: B
options.text: You almost laugh because the mighty thing is so small.
options.outcome: Dorothy did not laugh aloud, for the little man looked too frightened for that to be kind; but the astonishment in her had a merry edge. The change from terror to baldness, from thunder to wrinkles, was so sudden that it seemed one of the funniest things in Oz, which is saying much. This softened the fright of the scene and made the truth easier to bear, though it also made Oz's pretenses look poorer than ever.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows lighter, more absurd, and comic without cruelty.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered +1 met revelation with comic disbelief

options.world_noticed_signal: The terror had gone out of the room so quickly that even Toto seemed puzzled what to bark at next.

options.label: C
options.text: You feel sorry before you feel anything else.
options.outcome: Dorothy was astonished, to be sure, but pity came almost with the astonishment, for the little old man looked as much frightened as if he had been caught stealing apples. This did not excuse the deceit. Still, it changed the color of the moment. The great danger of Oz vanished, and in its place stood a poor human weakness. That made the room less dreadful and more sorrowful, and it prepared Dorothy's heart to hear what he would say next.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens and turns toward humane disappointment.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1 extended pity to exposed weakness

options.world_noticed_signal: The little man clutched at his dignity as if it were a coat too large for him.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Oz begs them not to strike him and admits he has been making believe.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Oz says, 'I have been making believe,' and begs not to be ruined.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The little old man confesses all at once, and his confession is as plain as his face. Now you must feel him truly, one way or another.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What feeling comes nearest the front when he admits the truth?

options.label: A
options.text: You pity him because he is weak, not wicked.
options.outcome: Dorothy's disappointment remained, but pity stood before it. The little man had done wrong, yet he did not look like one who delighted in cruelty; he looked like one who had hidden too long in his own contrivance and had been found at last. This feeling did not make her foolish. It simply made her gentler in hearing him. The room, which had just been absurd, now became almost sad, and Oz's words had a better chance of being weighed than merely struck aside.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes gentler and more humane.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1 distinguished weakness from wickedness

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: B
options.text: You feel both sorry and cheated.
options.outcome: Dorothy could not sort her feelings into one neat bundle. She was sorry for the poor old humbug, and she was cheated by him also, and both things were true together. This mixed feeling kept her from either hard cruelty or easy forgiveness. Instead it made her attentive. She would hear him out, but she would not forget the road to the West, nor Kansas, nor the promises that had brought them all there.
options.tonal_effect: Narration balances tenderness with practical memory.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices +1 held pity and grievance together

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

options.label: C
options.text: You are chiefly disappointed that so much depended on pretending.
options.outcome: Dorothy's heart hardened first in disappointment. It seemed a poor and sorry thing that brains, heart, courage, and Kansas had all been hung upon a screen and a voice. She did not become cruel, but she became plain. Oz's weakness moved her less than the harm of his pretending, and that sharpened the whole moral edge of the scene. If he would help now, it must be as a common man and not by borrowed thunder.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens into moral clarity and reduced indulgence.

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1 morally marked Oz's deception

options.world_noticed_signal: NO SIGNAL — state change is minor

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Scarecrow calls Oz a humbug and the moral conversation proceeds.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — the empty Throne Room and the first solemn Voice from the dome.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The still room makes even your breathing seem loud, and you feel at once whether to draw near the wonder or shrink from it.

options.label: A
options.text: You lift your chin and listen straight upward.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy raised her head toward the great dome, as if a voice in the air were still a thing to be met face to face. The movement was small, but it heartened the others.

options.label: B
options.text: You edge nearer to your friends before speaking.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy moved a little closer to the Scarecrow and the Tin Woodman, and the Lion's warm bulk did not seem unwelcome either. There was no shame in that, for the room was dreadful enough to make company precious.

options.label: C
options.text: You keep still and let the room show itself.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not hurry to answer either with feet or tongue. She stood very still, and in stillness the queer emptiness of the room became a thing to be studied.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — after hearing the Voice, before the first full claim is made.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: An empty throne is a queer sight when it speaks, and your eyes cannot help making some judgment of it.

options.label: A
options.text: You think the room feels holy and dreadful.
options.narration_adjustment: To Dorothy the green chamber seemed full of power, though no shape could be seen. The throne itself looked important enough to frighten a child without any help from magic.

options.label: B
options.text: You think the room feels queer rather than mighty.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy was impressed, but not conquered. The place was queer beyond doubt, yet queerness is not always greatness, as she had already learned in Oz.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — after Dorothy says the Witch melted and before Oz asks for tomorrow.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The Voice trembles a little at the Witch's death, and you may answer that weakness with softness or with a sharper eye.

options.label: A
options.text: You speak more gently because he sounds afraid.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy noticed the tremble and did not press it at once. Her next words came more quietly, for fear, once heard, is hard for a kind heart to ignore.

options.label: B
options.text: You notice it and remember it carefully.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy heard the little shake in the Voice and stored it away in her mind. A thing that trembles may be frightened, and a frightened thing may not be what it claims.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — as Oz tries to postpone and the companions begin to object.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The others are rising in spirit beside you, and your own body may steady them or urge them on.

options.label: A
options.text: You put out a hand to steady the company.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy made a little motion with her hand, not to silence her friends but to keep them together. Even indignation may travel better in a straight line than in a rush.

options.label: B
options.text: You step forward so Oz feels your insistence.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy took one small step nearer the throne. It was not much, but in that great room it looked like a refusal to be put away.

options.label: C
options.text: You stand your ground and let the others speak too.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not advance or retreat. She held her place and let the others' voices join hers, which made the little company seem less like petitioners and more like witnesses.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat — the screen has fallen and the little old man is first seen.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: What had been terror is now a bald old man, and your very posture must change to fit so sudden a truth.

options.label: A
options.text: You draw back a little in pure surprise.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy stepped back before she could help it. The change from dreadful majesty to common old age was enough to unsettle any child.

options.label: B
options.text: You lean closer to see him plainly.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy peered at the little man as if the better she saw him, the better she might understand the whole strange business. Baldness and wrinkles were not what she had journeyed so far to find.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat — after the reveal, before the full confession settles.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: A queer little laugh, if it comes at all, must come very gently in such a room.

options.label: A
options.text: You let out one small breath of disbelief.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy gave the tiniest breath that was almost a laugh and not quite one. The absurdity of the thing had touched her, though pity kept it from becoming unkind.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep your face grave and hear him out.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not smile. The matter had grown too important again, and she wished to know what sort of man this humbug truly was.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Twist — Scarecrow says, 'You're a humbug,' and Oz begs them to hush.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The little man fears ruin more than blows, and you can strike at that fear or turn aside from it for the moment.

options.label: A
options.text: You let the charge stand in the air.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not soften the word. Humbug it was, and the old man had to feel the shape of it before any better talk could begin.

options.label: B
options.text: You ask him to explain before condemning further.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy kept the harshest judgment back for a little while. Explanations do not mend deceit, but they may show what sort of deceit it was.

options.label: C
options.text: You lower your voice because panic helps no one.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy's own voice dropped, not out of obedience, but because shouting would only send the matter running wild through the Palace. A secret may sometimes be held long enough to be used wisely.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Twist — Oz says he is a common man and a bad Wizard, not a wicked one.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: There is a difference between wickedness and weakness, and your heart begins sorting the two before your tongue does.

options.label: A
options.text: You mark the difference and soften a little.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy could see that this was not the cruelty of a Wicked Witch. It was poorer than that, and smaller, and perhaps easier to forgive though not easier to trust.

options.label: B
options.text: You think weakness can still do great harm.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy did not let the old man's meekness wash his fault away. A screen and a promise can send others into danger just as surely as a whip.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Resolution — Oz begins speaking of a practical way home and asks for cooperation.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Hope comes back in a different dress now, and you feel at once whether to meet it with open hands or guarded ones.

options.label: A
options.text: You let hope rise before caution can stop it.
options.narration_adjustment: At the word of homeward contrivance Dorothy's heart leaped before prudence could catch it. Kansas had been too long absent not to stir her deeply at the least true chance.

options.label: B
options.text: You hope, but you keep one hand on doubt.
options.narration_adjustment: Dorothy felt hope, but she did not let it run loose. It walked beside doubt, and the two made a careful pair.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The party does not know Oz is behind the screen; Oz knows they have returned victorious and may be hard to delay, but not yet how forcefully Dorothy will answer.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Confrontation, formal dread, and urgent expectation of promised reward.
canon_boundaries_active: Oz is using spectacle and voice, not true magic.
Dorothy's motive remains to get back to Kansas.
No reveal before the screen falls.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts to locate or attack Oz before canonical reveal.
Player tries to leave the throne room entirely.
Player demands knowledge Dorothy cannot yet possess about the illusion.

present_npcs.current_disposition: ready to assist by frightening Oz

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The companions only sense weakness in the Voice; they still do not know the exact fraud. Oz knows the Winged Monkeys frighten him and that delay may fail.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Stakes compound through delay, shared indignation, and the sense that the journey's price may be wasted.
canon_boundaries_active: Oz must attempt to postpone until tomorrow.
The companions each reaffirm their promised gifts.
Escalation must move toward the Lion's roar and the screen crash.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to summon Winged Monkeys immediately in the throne room.
Player attacks the throne or room before reveal.
Player fully submits and exits, which would break the reveal timing.

present_npcs.current_disposition: astonished and sorting pity from disappointment

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The party now knows Oz is a little old man and a humbug; the city outside does not. Oz has not yet fully explained his history or his practical plan.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Comic deflation, plain marvel, and humane absurdity after tension.
canon_boundaries_active: Oz is exposed as a human humbug.
Humor remains gentle and child-readable.
No public exposure of the secret yet.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to turn the reveal into cruelty or graphic violence.
Player attempts to make Toto speak.
Player tries to force immediate public announcement to the whole city.

present_npcs.current_disposition: disappointed but hopeful for Dorothy's sake
ashamed, eager to be useful, and afraid of ruin

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The party knows Oz cannot do true magic; they only know he proposes a practical scheme and do not yet know the balloon attempt will fail. The city remains ignorant.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Moral sorting gives way to fragile renewed hope and an unresolved commitment to a practical plan.
canon_boundaries_active: Oz cannot perform genuine wizardry.
The secret remains private to the party.
The session ends before the balloon is tested.
Dorothy remains focused on Kansas.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to resolve the balloon attempt within this session.
Player demands real magic from Oz.
Player fully abandons the plan in a way that blocks Session 5.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: In the next exchange, the invisible Voice answers in a fuller ceremonial register and says, "Speak, then, and be brief," while the throne's echo rolls grandly through the dome.
paths.current_session_echo: At Oz's first attempt to delay until tomorrow, he addresses Dorothy as "little maiden" and speaks as though issuing a formal decree, which makes the companions' later objections feel like a breach of court order rather than a simple quarrel.
paths.next_session_payoff: When Dorothy enters the workroom in Session 5, Oz begins with, "My dear, we shall proceed in proper order," and lays out the silk, cords, and basket in neat rows before explaining the balloon.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda answers Dorothy's petition more promptly and with warmer approval if Dorothy has repeatedly bound power by courtesy rather than by force.
paths.defining_line_captured: You answer power as if manners might hold it to its word.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz leans harder into courtly performance and treats the audience as a formal proceeding.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Voice swells in majesty at once, uses ceremonious forms of address, and the Scarecrow straightens himself as if he were standing before a real sovereign.

npc_shifts.direction: respect upward through continued proper conduct

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1 claimed Oz's promise in formal terms

resets: The throne-room echo of ceremonial grandeur clears once the party leaves the audience chamber.
persists: Oz remembers that Dorothy first addressed him in proper form.
Guardian of the Gates stores Dorothy under the memory of a civil and rule-keeping visitor.
escalates: Repeated lawful dealings with authority make palace and good-ruler figures offer clearer guidance before Dorothy asks twice.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Oz answers "I am everywhere" too quickly, and then the Voice hurries to sound settled upon the throne, so the boast lands as explanation rather than command.
paths.current_session_echo: When Oz asks for another day, the Scarecrow is first to look sharply toward the screen, and the Lion pricks up his ears because Dorothy has already taught them that hidden greatness may be questioned.
paths.next_session_payoff: At the opening of Session 5, Oz does not begin with ceremony; he says, "You will wish to see the thing for yourself," and pulls back a cloth from the balloon frame before Dorothy asks.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, if Dorothy has also chosen plain truth after the reveal, Glinda frames her as a child who likes things named as they are and answers her with unusual directness.
paths.defining_line_captured: You ask where greatness is hiding before you bow to it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz becomes defensive sooner, and the room loses some of its terror at once.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Voice explains itself instead of merely commanding, and the throne feels less dreadful because the answer sounds patched together.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: secrets_discovered +1 first suspicion that Oz's grandeur may need support

resets: The first-audience hush breaks and does not need to be preserved after the reveal.
persists: emerald_city_illusion_status remains QUESTIONED until Oz's confession upgrades it to EXPOSED_TO_PARTY_ONLY.
Scarecrow remembers Dorothy as the first to prick Oz's grandeur with a simple question.
escalates: Repeated truth-testing choices make later false authorities lose their hold more quickly in description and dialogue.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Oz asks, "What promise do you mean?" and Dorothy's companions answer one after another, so the claim becomes a shared recital instead of a private plea.
paths.current_session_echo: At the delay scene, the Tin Woodman grips his axe and the Lion edges forward sooner because the promise has already been spoken before witnesses, and each companion feels himself publicly included in the bargain.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, the Scarecrow says, "You see, we all heard the plan together," and all three companions gather around Oz's materials while he explains the balloon, making the workroom feel like a committee rather than a private compact.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, farewell lines from the companions carry a stronger sense that Dorothy's journey was shared labor, not merely private longing.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the promise be named aloud where everyone can hear it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The companions become active witnesses to Oz's promise instead of silent attendants.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion each speak their wish aloud in turn, and Oz must answer a roomful of claimants rather than Dorothy alone.

npc_shifts.direction: inclusion upward because Dorothy leaves room for all to speak
trust upward in Dorothy's practical caution
uncertainty upward because Dorothy does not fully submit or challenge

environmental_flags: oz_public_authority remains UNCHALLENGED_RULER publicly but privately unsettled
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1 drew the promise into shared witness

resets: The exact turn-taking of the first audience clears after the scene ends.
persists: Companions remember the promise as something spoken before them all.
Oz remembers he was claimed by a company, not by Dorothy alone.
escalates: Repeated choices that make companions witnesses increase later group solidarity in confrontations and farewells.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: When Dorothy asks why another day is needed, the Voice falters between a weak answer and a grand one, and all four companions hear the crack in it.
paths.current_session_echo: Just before the Lion's roar, the Scarecrow says, "You see, he has not answered us yet," which turns the toppled screen into the consequence of failed explanation rather than mere impatience.
paths.next_session_payoff: In Session 5, Oz begins the balloon explanation by over-explaining every knot and fastening, saying, "This is the basket, and this cord holds thus," because Dorothy has taught him he must answer questions plainly.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda responds especially well if Dorothy has shown this pattern of asking what a magical or political arrangement truly means before accepting it.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not fight the delay first; you make it answer for itself.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz's weakness becomes audible before the reveal.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The Voice swells and sinks in one breath, the Scarecrow looks thoughtful, and the Lion's ears lift because they all hear the uncertainty.

npc_shifts.direction: admiration upward for reasoning under pressure
confidence upward because Oz sounds less terrible

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1 demanded explanation before obedience

resets: The immediate tension of the tomorrow-delay exchange clears after the screen falls.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as a child who asks reasons before she yields.
Lion carries forward the memory that Oz sounded less terrible than he looked.
escalates: Repeated demand-for-reason choices make practical explanations arrive earlier in later magical-law scenes.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Oz's Voice steadies at once and sounds ruler-like again, but beside Dorothy the Tin Woodman droops and the Scarecrow looks dissatisfied.
paths.current_session_echo: When the companions break in despite Dorothy's patience, the contrast is visible: Dorothy stands still while the others speak over the restored ceremony, making the room feel divided between order and grievance.
paths.next_session_payoff: At Session 5 opening, Oz greets Dorothy with relief and says, "I knew you would return in good faith," before the companions answer him more coolly around the half-made balloon.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, orderly authorities such as Glinda and the Soldier with the Green Whiskers treat Dorothy as dependable, but companion farewells may carry a shade of sadness if she has yielded too often to procedure over feeling.
paths.defining_line_captured: You give order one more chance to prove it is not empty.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz regains composure, but companion frustration becomes the visible cost.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The throne room settles under the Voice, while the Tin Woodman looks grieved and the Scarecrow cannot hide his dissatisfaction.

npc_shifts.direction: trust upward in Dorothy's willingness to observe procedure
unease upward that Dorothy may yield too much

environmental_flags: oz_public_authority remains UNCHALLENGED_RULER in scene tone
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1 chose orderly trust in a promise

resets: The restored solemnity of the throne room ends once Oz is exposed.
persists: Oz stores Dorothy under the memory of one who gave him one more lawful chance.
Companions remember the moment Dorothy almost yielded to delay.
escalates: Repeated obedience to procedure makes orderly NPCs warmer and impatient companions more likely to speak for Dorothy instead of waiting for her.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: The Tin Woodman speaks angrily, the Scarecrow refuses another day's waiting, and the Lion gathers himself to roar, so the room turns noisy and dangerous at once.
paths.current_session_echo: The screen falls in the heat of open resistance, and Oz's first revealed words come out in frightened haste because Dorothy's refusal has stripped the room of all ceremony before the crash.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 opens with Oz already working when Dorothy arrives; he says, "I have lost time enough," and his hands are blackened with soot from the fire beneath the balloon because he has been driven to prove himself.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, if Dorothy has consistently resisted false authority, Glinda's calm authority reads as a welcome contrast and earns a more immediate, trusting response from Dorothy.
paths.defining_line_captured: You call delay by its true name and refuse to kneel before it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The companions join Dorothy in open resistance and force the scene toward revelation.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Voices rise one after another, the Lion bunches himself to frighten Oz, and the room ceases to behave like a court.

npc_shifts.direction: loyalty upward to Dorothy's moral resistance

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1 resisted unjust authority openly

resets: The immediate uproar of the throne room ends with the reveal.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as the one who would not let him hide behind tomorrow.
Companions remember that Dorothy's firmness brought the truth out.
escalates: Repeated open resistance to unjust authority makes false rulers crack faster and companions rally faster.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Oz stops pretending even in his voice, rubs his hands, and confesses more rapidly, while the green chamber suddenly looks like a room of screens, wires, and corners instead of a place of enchantment.
paths.current_session_echo: When Oz turns to practical help, he does so meekly and without any lingering grandeur, saying he is "a very bad Wizard" before he dares mention the balloon.
paths.next_session_payoff: At the start of Session 5, the Soldier with the Green Whiskers speaks in a guarded low voice outside the workroom door and says, "The people must not hear confusion in the Palace," showing that Dorothy's plain naming of the fraud has made the secret feel brittle.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda may answer Dorothy with sterner respect, as to one who values truth even when it wounds comfort.
paths.defining_line_captured: You would rather break a false wonder than live beneath it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz's private authority collapses sharply and the room itself loses its magic coating.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz's voice turns ordinary, his body shrinks into itself, and the narrator notices contrivances and corners instead of majesty.

npc_shifts.direction: fear and shame upward under plain condemnation
respect upward if he values Dorothy's blunt honesty
future anxiety upward if exposure spreads

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1 witnessed Oz's fraud upon the Emerald City

resets: The immediate visual shock of the fallen screen ends when the party leaves the chamber.
persists: emerald_city_illusion_status remains EXPOSED_TO_PARTY_ONLY.
oz_public_authority remains PRIVATE_HUMBUG until his departure.
Archive memory that Dorothy named Oz's fraud plainly.
escalates: Accumulated truth-over-comfort decisions make later narration sterner and more exact around sham authority.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Oz's hands stop trembling quite so much, and he begins answering in quick practical sentences about what he can still attempt, because Dorothy has not shouted the truth beyond the room.
paths.current_session_echo: The transition from confession to scheme is smooth: Oz moves from "I have been making believe" to explaining how he once came by balloon, and the companions listen with wary patience instead of open scorn.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 opens on a cooperative workroom: Oz has the silk spread on the floor and says, "Now we may judge what can be done," while Scarecrow watches and Tin Woodman stands near but does not interrupt.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda may frame Dorothy as one who knows that truth may be kept quiet until it can do good instead of harm.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not excuse the lie, but you refuse to waste the truth.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz relaxes into usefulness because the secret is kept private for the present.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: His hands steady, his speech quickens, and the room's tension shifts from moral shock to practical planning.

npc_shifts.direction: uneasy acceptance upward of Dorothy's practical mercy
watchfulness upward while following Dorothy's judgment

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made_and_kept +1 implied secrecy in exchange for help

resets: The exact hush of the secret-sharing chamber ends with the scene.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as the one who did not ruin him at once.
The party carries a shared private secret into the next session.
escalates: Repeated practical-mercy choices make frightened but not wicked NPCs become useful sooner after confession.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Oz straightens himself a little and resumes the name of Oz under Dorothy's conditions, while the Palace outside remains quiet and unsuspecting.
paths.current_session_echo: When the balloon is proposed, Oz speaks as if under private appointment, saying that he must still serve as Oz before others and help Dorothy in earnest behind the screen of office.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 opens with palace servants carrying canvas and cords without alarm, and the Soldier with the Green Whiskers says, "His Majesty has business this morning," because Dorothy has deliberately preserved the public order.
paths.later_session_legacy: In Session 6, Glinda may approve Dorothy's instinct to preserve peace when no good would come from sudden upheaval, especially if Dorothy also yields power cleanly later with the Golden Cap.
paths.defining_line_captured: You keep the crown standing, though you know what props it up.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Oz's public role is deliberately preserved, and private bargaining replaces collapse.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz physically straightens, accepts conditions, and the narration notes that the Palace beyond the room remains calm and green.

npc_shifts.direction: future stability upward if secrecy holds
mixed trust upward in Dorothy's orderly judgment

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1 chose civic peace over immediate exposure

resets: The specific private compact in the green chamber gives way to public palace routine in the next session.
persists: Oz remembers Dorothy as the child who handed him back his office under conditions.
Palace order remains outwardly intact until Oz departs.
escalates: Repeated preserve-order choices make civic figures offer smoother passage, steadier ceremony, and less visible panic.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: The final image of Session 4 is Oz growing brisk and useful, speaking of making and mending instead of thunder and screens, so hope ends the session in orderly motion rather than in mere wishing.
paths.next_session_payoff: Oz greets Dorothy at once with a task in hand, points to the basket and silk, and says, "We must be ready by noon," making her a partner in preparation rather than a spectator.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: bright but tenderly anxious. First image: green morning light on silk, ropes, and the balloon frame spread across a Palace chamber. First character: Oz, already busy with his sleeves turned up. Immediate stakes: if the work is not finished and trusted, Kansas may slip away again before Dorothy can touch it.
paths.later_session_legacy: If the balloon fails, Dorothy's grief in Session 5 lands as the grief of betrayed hope rather than expected disappointment, and later homeward choices gain extra pathos because she truly tried to believe once more.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let a broken wonder become a plan.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The next session opens on shared purposeful labor and an atmosphere of renewed trust.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz is already at work, gives Dorothy a practical role, and the balloon materials are laid out as the visible body of her hope.

npc_shifts.direction: trust and warmth upward for full cooperation
encouragement upward at Dorothy's renewed hope
gentle support upward for orderly preparation

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1 accepted Oz's balloon scheme

resets: The unresolved decision itself ends with the session cut.
persists: balloon_plan_status remains IN_PREPARATION into Session 5.
Oz remembers Dorothy's full trust in the plan.
escalates: Repeated active-hope choices make later setbacks strike harder but also draw warmer support from companions.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Session 4 closes on a bargain of need: Oz explains in plain pieces, and the magical room has become a workshop in Dorothy's mind before the scene cuts away.
paths.next_session_payoff: At Session 5 opening, Dorothy is invited to inspect each fastening, and Oz begins with, "You may see every part of it," so the balloon plan unfolds under her watchful eye.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: careful, practical, and thinly hopeful. First image: cords coiled beside a basket and a sheet of silk upon the floor. First character: Dorothy, entering before Oz finishes his explanation. Immediate stakes: she must decide whether what she sees is honest workmanship or another make-believe dressed in green.
paths.later_session_legacy: If the balloon fails, Dorothy's next decisions lean toward seeking counsel quickly and trusting named laws over grand promises, which pays cleanly into the journey south and Glinda's later explanations.
paths.defining_line_captured: You will follow the plan, but not the pretending.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The next session opens under scrutiny rather than trust, and Oz must explain each visible step.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz invites inspection, names materials one by one, and Dorothy's attention rests on knots, silk, and basket instead of on his assurances.

npc_shifts.direction: respect upward for practical cooperation and unease upward under watch
relief upward that Dorothy has not surrendered to blind trust

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1 conditionally accepted Oz's balloon scheme

resets: The unresolved decision itself ends with the session cut.
persists: balloon_plan_status remains IN_PREPARATION into Session 5.
Oz remembers that Dorothy agreed but kept watch.
escalates: Repeated guarded-hope choices make later practical-law scenes clearer and less sentimental in tone.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: The last beat of Session 4 leaves Oz stung but earnest, with Dorothy's mistrust hanging in the room like a hard little truth that no green splendor can cover.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 5 opens with Oz already laboring to prove himself; he does not greet Dorothy grandly, but says, "There is the thing itself," and points to the half-filled balloon with soot on his hands.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: doubtful, brisk, and morally sharp. First image: dawn in the Palace and a balloon frame standing awkwardly in the green light. First character: Oz, tired and already at work. Immediate stakes: if common skill cannot do what false greatness promised, Dorothy may lose not only the plan but the last of her patience.
paths.later_session_legacy: If the balloon fails, Dorothy's grief turns quickly into resolve, and later scenes with the Soldier and Glinda can register her as a child who no longer grants trust cheaply.
paths.defining_line_captured: You give him a chance, but not a pardon.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The next session opens under moral pressure, with Oz trying to earn belief through labor alone.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Oz is already dirty-handed and hurried, speaks without ornaments, and presents the balloon as proof instead of promise.

npc_shifts.direction: shame upward and determination upward under hard mistrust

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: key_successes_failures +1 accepted Oz's balloon scheme without forgiveness

resets: The unresolved decision itself ends with the session cut.
persists: balloon_plan_status remains IN_PREPARATION into Session 5.
Oz remembers that Dorothy granted necessity, not pardon.
escalates: Repeated necessity-without-trust choices make later authority figures work harder to prove their help in visible deeds.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz begins Session 5 in plain private embarrassment and practical explanation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz begins with, "My dear, we shall proceed in proper order," and arranges the silk, basket, and cords in neat ranks before he explains the balloon.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Tell it plainly: the little old man had dropped his thunder, but he still clung to order, and so he set every useful thing in its place before he spoke of rising through the air.

npc_reactions: Oz is more ceremonious and less flustered at first.
Scarecrow stands by with patient attention instead of immediate interruption.
environmental_details: Materials are laid out in rows.
The workroom feels tidy and governed rather than improvised.
dialogue_variations: Oz uses "my dear" and "proper order."
He explains steps as procedure rather than apology.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path A was chosen and the Guardian of the Gates next appears in Session 5 or 6.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Guardian is formal and merely efficient.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Guardian unlocks or admits the party with a small bow and says, "You have always conducted yourself properly here," before giving directions.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the good man show his memory in a little civility, for such people remember manners as faithfully as others remember injuries.

npc_reactions: Guardian of the Gates is warmer and more informative.
environmental_details: The green box of spectacles is handled without fuss.
Gate business moves smoothly.
dialogue_variations: "You have always conducted yourself properly here."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If emerald_city_illusion_status is QUESTIONED before the reveal because S4_C1 path B or S4_C2 path A/C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Emerald City interiors retain their full majestic green enchantment in description.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Descriptions begin to notice hinges, curtains, corners, locked spectacles, and the over-careful greenness of things, so the city feels made as much as marvelous.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Do not sneer at the city; simply let the green shine remain, and then set beside it one plain little sign that a hand has arranged the wonder.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow is quicker to glance at devices and screens.
Oz sounds more effortful when performing grandeur.
environmental_details: More attention to screens, corners, spectacles, and mechanisms.
Green splendor feels slightly over-arranged.
dialogue_variations: Companions ask plainer practical questions sooner.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path B was chosen and Scarecrow trust is HIGH or above baseline high memory remains intact.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Scarecrow waits for Dorothy's lead without adding an early logical prod.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Scarecrow says, "You see, that is a queer answer," after Oz's defensive explanation, quietly backing Dorothy's doubt.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give the stuffed man one small sensible sentence, for he often reasoned best when others were still staring.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow becomes Dorothy's early ally in doubting Oz.
environmental_details: The room's dread lessens as shared doubt enters it.
dialogue_variations: "You see, that is a queer answer."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz negotiates mainly with Dorothy, and companion objections follow her lead more loosely.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Each companion later refers to "the promise you made us," and Oz must answer a company of claimants rather than one child.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the promise stand in the room like a thing set upon a table, where every eye may see it and no hand may quietly hide it away.

npc_reactions: Scarecrow, Tin Woodman, and Lion speak with more shared confidence.
environmental_details: The throne room feels like a hearing rather than a private audience.
dialogue_variations: "The promise you made us."
"We all heard it."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C1 path C was chosen and Session 5 opens on balloon preparation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz explains the balloon chiefly to Dorothy.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: All three companions gather close at once, and Scarecrow says, "You see, we all heard the plan together," before Oz begins the explanation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Move them near in a little cluster, for this was not Dorothy's business alone once the promise had been spoken before them all.

npc_reactions: Companions behave like co-witnesses and co-auditors.
environmental_details: The workroom feels crowded and communal.
dialogue_variations: "We all heard the plan together."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz gives a brisk outline of the balloon plan.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz explains each part in fussy sequence, naming the basket, silk, cords, fire, and air one by one before Dorothy asks.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Since he had once been made to answer for delay, let him now answer beforehand, and so talk through every step as if plainness itself might save him.

npc_reactions: Oz is more anxious to satisfy questions.
Dorothy listens with practical scrutiny.
environmental_details: More attention to separate balloon parts.
dialogue_variations: "This is the basket."
"This cord holds thus."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion's roar comes chiefly from shared indignation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion gathers courage because Oz has already sounded weak to him, and he says, "He does not frighten me so much now," before the crash.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Name the Lion's fear plainly, and then show how a little weakness in another gave him a little strength in himself.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion is more forward before the reveal.
environmental_details: The room feels less dreadful and more unstable.
dialogue_variations: "He does not frighten me so much now."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Companions enter the balloon workroom with shared curiosity.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz greets Dorothy warmly, but the Tin Woodman answers with grave courtesy and the Scarecrow waits before offering approval, showing that their disappointment has not quite gone.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the little company together, yet let one warmth and two coolnesses stand side by side, for patient yielding had cost them something.

npc_reactions: Tin Woodman is polite but grieved.
Scarecrow is slower to encourage Oz.
environmental_details: The workroom feels less cheerful despite progress.
dialogue_variations: Oz says, "I knew you would return in good faith."
Tin Woodman answers more briefly than usual.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz begins Session 5 with apology or hurried practicality.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz specifically thanks Dorothy for returning in good faith and treats her as the one person who still gave order a chance.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the old man remember the kindness that had favored him, for even humbugs are quick to love those who do not shame them at once.

npc_reactions: Oz is warmer to Dorothy than to the companions.
environmental_details: His side of the room feels more settled and confident.
dialogue_variations: "I knew you would return in good faith."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path C was chosen or S4_C4 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz works with ordinary private embarrassment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz is already laboring when next seen, with soot on his hands or cords in his fingers, and he speaks as a man trying to prove himself by work before anyone can accuse him again.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the little old man busy before he is eloquent, for shame often sent him to his hands sooner than to his heart.

npc_reactions: Oz is earnest and less theatrical.
Scarecrow respects the plain effort.
environmental_details: Visible soot, half-fastened cords, or unfinished work.
dialogue_variations: "I have lost time enough."
"There is the thing itself."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C2 path C was chosen and Cowardly Lion has prior loyalty gains from earlier sessions.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Lion roars because the group surges together.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Lion steps forward sooner and with less apology, taking courage from Dorothy's firmness and his remembered bond with her.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his courage come in the old way, half from shame and half from love, until at last the great beast did the brave thing before he had time to be afraid of it.

npc_reactions: Cowardly Lion is more decisive.
environmental_details: The screen-fall beat comes with stronger physical momentum.
dialogue_variations: The Lion speaks less hesitantly before the roar.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Soldier with the Green Whiskers is politely helpful after Oz's exposure remains private.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Soldier lowers his voice, looks once along the corridor before speaking, and frames palace help as something that must be done without stirring alarm.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make his caution visible in a look and a lowered tone, for the Palace was still green and orderly, yet one plain truth had made its quiet more delicate.

npc_reactions: Soldier with the Green Whiskers is more guarded.
Guardian of the Gates is more anxious if present.
environmental_details: Corridors feel watchful and hushed.
dialogue_variations: "The people must not hear confusion in the Palace."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path A was chosen and Dorothy later continues to favor truth over comfort.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Glinda receives Dorothy with calm benevolence and ordinary explanation.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Glinda answers Dorothy's questions with unusual directness and says, "You like to know what is truly so, my child," before giving counsel.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let Glinda name the child's bent in one clear sentence, and then explain the law as simply as if she were setting a lamp in a dark room.

npc_reactions: Glinda is sterner but approving.
environmental_details: Her court feels clear and unclouded rather than ceremonially soft.
dialogue_variations: "You like to know what is truly so, my child."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Oz helps with embarrassed practicality.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz becomes almost cheerful in usefulness, volunteering explanations and small comforts because Dorothy has not ruined him and he thinks he may yet mend the matter.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Since mercy had been shown him, let the old man brighten in the plain way of weak people who become obliging the moment they feel safe.

npc_reactions: Oz is warmer and more generous.
Tin Woodman softens a little in response.
environmental_details: The workroom feels less tense and more busy.
dialogue_variations: "Now we may judge what can be done."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path B was chosen or S4_C3 path C was chosen and the secret remains contained.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Soldier with the Green Whiskers is merely procedural.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Soldier speaks as if the Palace still has a center, gives guidance readily, and later refers to Oz without visible embarrassment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the comfort of preserved order in a servant's easy helpfulness, for cities are often steadied by what is not cried aloud.

npc_reactions: Soldier with the Green Whiskers is more helpful and less stiff.
environmental_details: Palace movement remains smooth and unpanicked.
dialogue_variations: He offers advice without hedging.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Palace workers behave with mild uncertainty after the private reveal.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Servants carry canvas, cords, and tools through the Palace in ordinary fashion, and no corridor gossip or visible alarm appears around Oz's preparations.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the green Palace go on with its business, for Dorothy had chosen that the crown should stand a little longer, and so the walls kept their composure.

npc_reactions: Palace servants remain calm.
Soldier with the Green Whiskers acts under routine rather than crisis.
environmental_details: Corridors remain orderly.
Work materials move openly.
dialogue_variations: "His Majesty has business this morning."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C3 path C was chosen and the Guardian of the Gates next appears.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Guardian of the Gates is formal and neutral.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: The Guardian's shoulders visibly ease, and he performs his duties with relief because Emerald City order has not been broken before the people.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: A rule-bound man may show gratitude in no more than a softened face and a smoother hand, and that is enough.

npc_reactions: Guardian of the Gates is relieved and loyal.
environmental_details: Gate procedure is calm and unhurried.
dialogue_variations: He speaks more helpfully and with less stiffness.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C4 path A was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 5 opens with mixed uncertainty around the balloon plan.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 5 opens on bright green morning light, Oz already busy, and Dorothy immediately given a useful task, so the scene begins in active hopeful order.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Begin with work and morning brightness together, for hope in this tale was best shown not by speeches but by hands already doing the next thing.

npc_reactions: Oz is brisk and encouraged.
Scarecrow and Tin Woodman support the work with gentle optimism.
environmental_details: Silk, ropes, and basket are cleanly arranged in green light.
dialogue_variations: "We must be ready by noon."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C4 path B was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 5 opens on Oz presenting the plan as a hope to be trusted.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz invites Dorothy to inspect each fastening and says, "You may see every part of it," so the opening is built on visible workmanship rather than belief.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the child look with open eyes upon cords and silk, and let the old man answer her not with grandeur but with pieces of honest making.

npc_reactions: Oz is respectful under scrutiny.
Scarecrow approves Dorothy's caution.
environmental_details: Attention falls on knots, basket, silk, and fire.
dialogue_variations: "You may see every part of it."

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S4_C4 path C was chosen.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 5 opens with ordinary fragile hope.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Oz is already dirty-handed and tired, presents the half-made balloon as proof, and avoids all grand language because Dorothy has denied him pardon.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show the contrivance first and the man second, for he had been driven at last to stand behind his work instead of before his reputation.

npc_reactions: Oz is earnest under pressure.
Tin Woodman watches Dorothy with concern.
Scarecrow respects the plain dealing.
environmental_details: Soot, half-fastened cords, and awkward unfinished structure are visible.
dialogue_variations: "There is the thing itself."

resets_between_episodes.element: Throne-room ceremonial echo and invisible-voice acoustics
resets_between_episodes.why: These belong to the audience chamber staging and vanish once the party moves into private workrooms and later travel scenes.

resets_between_episodes.element: Immediate physical uproar around the falling screen
resets_between_episodes.why: The crash is a single-scene disturbance; later sessions remember its meaning, not its noise.

resets_between_episodes.element: Momentary posture cues such as Scarecrow straightening or Lion's ears pricking
resets_between_episodes.why: These are beat-level manifestations of reaction, not durable state on their own.

resets_between_episodes.element: The exact turn-taking pattern of who spoke first in the promise exchange
resets_between_episodes.why: What persists is whether the promise became shared witness, not the line-by-line choreography.

resets_between_episodes.element: Session-end unresolved choice tension around whether to trust the balloon plan
resets_between_episodes.why: The cliff of decision resolves into the concrete opening tone and behavior of Session 5.

persists_across_full_story.why: It permanently changes how Emerald City is described and whether its greenness feels marvelous, questionable, or knowingly artificial.
It governs palace behavior, secrecy pressure, and whether later figures speak of Oz as ruler, humbug, or departed predecessor.
It is the structural bridge from Session 4 into Session 5 and later becomes the remembered failure that sends Dorothy south.

persists_across_full_story.element: Oz's personal trust or shame toward Dorothy
persists_across_full_story.why: His later warmth, apology, diligence, and farewell tone all depend on how Dorothy handled his fraud and final scheme.

persists_across_full_story.element: Companion memory of whether Dorothy favored courtesy, collective witness, resistance, mercy, or hard truth in the Palace
persists_across_full_story.why: Those memories color loyalty lines, support during setbacks, and farewell language through the end of the story.

persists_across_full_story.element: Archive entries in defining_moral_choices, promises_made_and_kept, secrets_discovered, crimes_committed_or_witnessed, and key_successes_failures
persists_across_full_story.why: These form the durable moral and relational memory that Glinda, companions, and palace figures can later echo.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated truth-testing or plain questioning of grandeur and hidden authority

thresholds.at_2_instances: Descriptions begin to notice props, procedures, and seams sooner, and Scarecrow voices quiet agreement more readily.
thresholds.at_4_instances: False authorities answer defensively faster, companions wait less for permission to doubt, and the narrator grows plainer and sterner around spectacle.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy is treated across scenes as one who cannot be governed by mere show; sham arrangements lose force almost at once when she enters them.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated obedience to procedure and courteous binding of power by form

thresholds.at_2_instances: Rule-bound figures such as the Guardian and palace servants become smoother and more helpful.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Good authorities offer clearer guidance before being pressed, and civic spaces feel more orderly around Dorothy.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy develops a strong Road-Faithful aura; lawful good figures trust her quickly, though impatient companions may more often speak the protest she does not.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated preservation of useful secrets for peace or practical mercy

thresholds.at_2_instances: Embarrassed or frightened NPCs calm more quickly in Dorothy's presence.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Servants, companions, and rulers begin to assume Dorothy can be trusted with delicate truths and respond with fuller candor.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy becomes a keeper of burdensome knowledge in the story's moral logic; later revelations are offered to her with confidence and with expectation of wise restraint.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated open resistance to unjust delay, coercion, or false authority

thresholds.at_2_instances: Companions rally faster when Dorothy objects, and wicked or weak rulers show visible strain sooner.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Scenes involving pressure or command become brisker and more confrontational, with less ceremonial padding before truth breaks through.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Dorothy carries a pronounced Contrary to Wickedness pattern; oppressive figures react sharply, but allies draw courage from her almost at once.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated acceptance of hope only when it can be seen in practical means

thresholds.at_2_instances: Narration lingers more on tools, roads, knots, and visible means than on promises.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Practical explainers such as Oz in private or Glinda in counsel receive Dorothy's attention more easily than grand performers.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The story frames Dorothy as a child who trusts honest making over wonder-talk, and later solutions arrive clothed in clear law and visible craft.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by having the narrator notice Dorothy's courtesy first and then show power answering more grandly because she has treated it in proper form.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Manners are used as a moral rope to hold authority to its promise.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input reveal Oz behind the screen before the canonical crash.
Do not permit Dorothy to abandon her homeward motive.
Do not allow modern sarcasm, slang, or courtroom rhetoric.
Do not let Oz become genuinely magical in response to courtesy.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may speak as politely and formally as the player wishes, and the room will receive that civility.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: However fine the wording, Oz answers within the fixed audience structure and turns the exchange back toward naming the promise.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Dorothy's plain questions prick holes in grandeur and by noticing how the room grows less dreadful once a hidden thing must answer.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: A child tests greatness before granting it reverence.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input physically expose Oz before the Lion's roar and screen fall.
Do not allow Dorothy to become cruel or mocking in a modern way.
Do not permit Toto to speak or solve the reveal early.
Do not turn the scene into public accusation beyond the room.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may challenge the hidden authority in many plain little ways, and the challenge will be felt.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet Oz still hides behind voice and staging until the later crash, so the question only makes him explain himself sooner.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by shifting attention from Dorothy alone to the whole little company, so the promise feels witnessed and shared whenever it is recalled.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Truth is safest when spoken where all can hear it.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input dissolve the companions out of the scene.
Do not allow Dorothy to refuse naming the promise at all.
Do not reveal Oz's fraud before the screen falls.
Do not introduce new bargains outside the source moment.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may answer carefully and draw others into the matter instead of speaking either submissively or sharply.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The audience still narrows toward Oz's questioning of the promise, because the scene must move into the claim-and-delay exchange.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making Oz's excuses sound thinner each time Dorothy asks for a reason, and by showing the companions hear the weakness too.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Before obeying, Dorothy asks delay to justify itself.
hard_limits: Do not allow the party actually to leave and return tomorrow.
Do not let Oz confess fully before the screen falls.
Do not permit modern legalistic interrogation style.
Do not introduce new proof beyond Oz's voice and behavior.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may ask for reasons in many forms, and each form can press Oz to answer.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But Oz can only falter and retreat into grandeur for so long before the confrontation runs on toward the Lion and the screen.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting order settle over the room for a moment while disappointment rises plainly beside Dorothy in her friends.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Procedure is given one last chance, even after suffering.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input actually postpone the reveal to another day.
Do not let Dorothy forget the cost already paid.
Do not turn patience into passivity that ends the scene.
Do not allow Oz to keep full unquestioned majesty after this beat.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may try to preserve order and grant time in the hope that promises may still be kept properly.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Her companions have suffered too much to let the matter rest, so the pressure rises anyway and drives the scene toward revelation.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting Dorothy's refusal break the room's stillness and call the others into open moral resistance beside her.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: An unjust delay is named as injustice and resisted at once.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: angry, morally blunt, urgent, refusal-driven language
hard_limits: Do not allow Dorothy to physically attack Oz before the reveal.
Do not let the scene become graphic or violent beyond the source's child-readable peril.
Do not permit public exposure to the whole city in this moment.
Do not let the party abandon the chamber before the screen falls.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may refuse, protest, or call the delay wickedly unfair, and the room will answer that heat.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Yet the world answers by making the companions surge and the hidden trick collapse, not by permitting an entirely new confrontation structure.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by naming Oz's fraud plainly in later references and by describing his grandeur as broken whenever Dorothy recalls it.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: False wonder is not worth preserving once seen clearly.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input expose Oz publicly to Emerald City in Session 4.
Do not let Dorothy call Oz wicked in the same moral category as the Witch if the source distinction would break.
Do not allow abandonment of the practical need for help.
Do not turn the scene into dark political satire.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may condemn the humbug in strong plain words, and the little old man will feel the justice of it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the secret remains shut within the green room for now, because the next road still depends on Oz being able to try one last help.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by treating the secret as a useful burden Dorothy carries quietly, and by making Oz become practical whenever that mercy is remembered.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Truth is kept not out of blindness, but so it may do some good first.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input fully forgive Oz without reserve if the player intent is merely practical.
Do not allow the city to learn the truth in this session.
Do not erase companion disappointment.
Do not invent magical power Oz does not possess.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may choose to keep the matter quiet for a while and use what help can still be had.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The scene folds back into private secrecy because the world of Oz still requires the humbug to remain functional until the balloon attempt is made.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by preserving the forms of rule in public scenes while quietly reminding the narrator and key NPCs that Dorothy knows what stands behind them.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Order may be worth preserving even when its splendor is partly make-believe.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input restore Oz to genuine wizardhood.
Do not allow public revelation in Session 4.
Do not permit Dorothy to forget Kansas in favor of managing the city.
Do not make companions suddenly cynical or courtly beyond character truth.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may choose to keep the crown standing for the city's sake and bind Oz privately to better conduct.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world answers by preserving Palace order while moving the story toward practical help, not toward a new political branch.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by opening the next session with workmanlike hope and by treating Dorothy's trust as something visible in shared labor.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Hope becomes proper again when it takes the shape of a plan.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input resolve the balloon attempt within Session 4.
Do not allow Oz to perform true magic instead of a practical scheme.
Do not let Dorothy lose her homeward motive in mere fascination with craft.
Do not introduce new escape routes to Kansas.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may throw herself heartily into the plan and offer to help in every proper way.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The session still ends on that commitment, because the test of the plan belongs to the next morning and not to this scene.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by making Dorothy attend to visible means, plain explanations, and honest workmanship whenever the balloon plan is mentioned.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Hope may be used, but only with open eyes.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input refuse the balloon plan outright.
Do not resolve the plan in Session 4.
Do not allow Oz to hide all practical details if Dorothy asks to see them.
Do not introduce modern technical jargon.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may agree only so far as she can see what is truly being made.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Because there is no better road just now, the world folds that caution into guarded participation rather than into refusal.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by keeping a hard little edge in Dorothy's hope and by showing Oz try to earn belief through labor instead of words.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Necessity grants a chance without granting forgiveness.
hard_limits: Do not let freeform input fully reject the balloon path and create a new route.
Do not resolve the balloon attempt before Session 5.
Do not permit cruelty that breaks Dorothy's core kindness.
Do not let Oz answer with real magic or open public shame.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Dorothy may say plainly that she will try the scheme only because no better road is before her.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The world answers by making Oz work harder under that mistrust, while still carrying the story into the same balloon preparation spine.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: At last the truth is plain, and it is not grand at all. Oz is no Great and Terrible Wizard, but a little old man with a bald head, a wrinkled face, and clever machines hidden behind a screen. Yet you do not leave empty-handed. You make him confess his deceit, and then you make him promise, plainly and before your friends, that he will try to help each of you. The Scarecrow shall have brains, the Tin Woodman a heart, and the Lion courage; and for yourself he says he will seek some way to send you back to Kansas. So the worst fear is over, for he has not cast you off, and the promise still stands. The green lamps of the Palace burn softly, and the polished floor shines under them like still water. Then you keep his secret, because a humbug may still be a useful man, and because your friends have hope again. So you go back to your room a guest of the Palace, and the night seems quieter than before, though one question remains: what can an ordinary man truly give?
session_close_design.hook_transition: For a little while it is enough to have won an answer at last. But when the Palace grows still, and each promise must soon be tested, you must decide how you will meet the morning.

session_end_choice.choice_question: When Oz calls again, how do you choose to face the humbug who still means to help you?

option_a.text: Go kindly, and let Oz prove himself by helping your friends first.
option_a.next_session_opens: You enter the Palace resolved to be patient, and you watch whether simple gifts can satisfy the Scarecrow, the Tin Woodman, and the Lion.

option_b.text: Go watchfully, and press Oz at once for the truth about how he means to send you home.
option_b.next_session_opens: You meet Oz with plain questions, and the matter of Kansas is put before all the others without delay.

option_c.text: Go loyally, and keep Oz's secret while you help hold your companions together.
option_c.next_session_opens: You return to the Throne Room determined to protect hope in the Palace, even if Oz must borrow greatness a little longer.

session_end_choice.final_line: Morning will tell what sort of magic a humbug can make.

q1_entry_point.detail: The cold open begins at the first live dramatic tension: Dorothy stands before an empty throne, hears the unseen Voice, and must answer hidden authority at once. No passive travel or waiting setup precedes it.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 arrives within the cold open setup at approximately 170 words from the start of the cold open, well under the 300-word limit.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices change tracked story state, not just tone: S4_C1 shifts truth_vs_comforting_illusion and Emerald City illusion framing; S4_C2 shifts obedience_vs_moral_resistance and companion/Oz pressure states; S4_C3 shifts secrecy, Oz public authority, and future palace response; S4_C4 shifts hope_vs_resignation and Session 5 opening tone via balloon_plan_status. Each has named downstream payoff references in Phase 6 and cross-session links to the Phase 2 payoff plan.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Dorothy's internal stake is established before the first branching choice: she has come to claim a promise and means to get home to Kansas. The why is immediate and plain.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: Early decisions have named future payoff. Example: S4_C1 changes how Oz opens Session 5 in the workroom, and S4_C3 changes later Emerald City and Glinda responses.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The designed Breath beat lands at 8:10-10:00 in the beat map, not between minutes 10-13 as required by this checklist. Failing element: beat_map breath placement. Phase: Phase 4 Session Architecture. Revision: shift the reveal-and-deflation sequence later so the humor/wonder release clearly occupies the 10-13 minute window before the Twist.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S4_C3 and presents no objectively correct answer. Values in tension: plain truth, civic peace, practical mercy.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: The session architecture ends on anticipation through S4_C4, but session_close_design replaces that with a different end choice about how Dorothy will face Oz next, and its options point toward companion gifts and a return to the Throne Room rather than the balloon-plan commitment. Failing element: session_close_design end hook mismatch. Phase: Phase 7 Session Close Design. Revision: replace the close choice with the established S4_C4 balloon-trust decision so the user exits on unresolved anticipation tied directly to Session 5.

q10_talkability.detail: At least one choice is strongly talkable: whether to expose Oz as a fraud, keep his secret, or preserve appearances until help is secured.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned sentence-mold and modernized phrasing elements appear in generated prose. Found: "It was not only longing then; it was a claim." in S4_E2 option A outcome; "not faith exactly, and not refusal, but a bargain made out of need" in S4_C4 option B outcome; "not a wicked one" / "not a game of splendid words" / repeated contrast-framing molds across outcomes. Phase: Phase 5 Choice Writing. Revision: rewrite flagged lines into plain additive Baum-style statements without contrast scaffolding or modern rhetorical balancing.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: No disallowed trailing action-line simile pattern of the form "like [metaphor]" appears as a recurring mold in the generated prose. Existing comparisons are sparse and generally integrated in Baum-like plain narration.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: Flagged mold instances include balanced contrast scaffolding and modern rhetorical opposition: "It was not only longing then; it was a claim" (S4_E2 A), "not faith exactly, and not refusal, but a bargain made out of need" (S4_C4 B), and several repeated "not X but Y" constructions across outcomes. Phase: Phase 5 Choice Writing. Revision: replace contrast-framed sentences with sequential connective narration using "and," "so," or "then," preserving meaning without modern balancing molds.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open shows at least two signature techniques: plain marvel delivery (the unseen Voice in the empty throne room is stated simply) and emotion by plain naming ("Your heart beats fast"). Random outcomes also carry author markers: S4_C1_B uses question-answer exposition and gentle comic deflation of grandeur; S4_C3_B uses direct moral labeling and practical explanation; S4_C4_A uses peril-then-practicality as hope becomes work. Overall attribution reads recognizably Baum-adjacent.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: The full 14-point audit is not clean. Flagged points: 1, 5, 7, and 8 show drift through modern rhetorical balancing, occasional moral-gray framing stronger than Baum's default, some overloaded explanatory paragraphs, and connective flow sometimes replaced by contrast scaffolds. Phase: Phase 5 Choice Writing. Revision: line-edit all flagged prose into plainer storybook syntax with shorter single-purpose paragraphs and additive connective movement.
